text.NRSV: At that time Joshua came and wiped out the Anakim from the hill country, from Hebron, from Debir, from Anab, and from all the hill country of Judah, and from all the hill country of Israel; Joshua utterly destroyed them with their towns.
text.Rotherham: And Joshua came in, at that time, and cut off the Anakim from the hill country—from Hebron, from Debir, from Anab, and from all the hill country of Judah, and from all the hill country of Israel,—with their cities, did Joshua devote them to destruction.
text.KJV: And at that time came Joshua, and cut off the Anakims from the mountains, from Hebron, from Debir, from Anab, and from all the mountains of Judah, and from all the mountains of Israel: Joshua destroyed them utterly with their cities.
text.NABRE: At that time Joshua penetrated the mountain regions and exterminated the Anakim in Hebron, Debir, Anab, the entire mountain region of Judah, and the entire mountain region of Israel. Joshua put them and their cities under the ban,
text.DRB: At that time Josue came and slew the Enacims of the mountains, of Hebron, and Dabir, and Anab, and from all the mountain of Juda and Israel, and destroyed their cities.

text.NRSV: None of the Anakim was left in the land of the Israelites; some remained only in Gaza, in Gath, and in Ashdod.
text.Rotherham: There was left remaining none of the Anakim, in the land of the sons of Israel,—save only, in Gaza, in Gath, and in Ashdod, did some remain.
text.KJV: There was none of the Anakims left in the land of the children of Israel: only in Gaza, in Gath, and in Ashdod, there remained.
text.NABRE: so that no Anakim were left in the land of the Israelites. However, some survived in Gaza, in Gath, and in Ashdod.
text.DRB: He left not any of the stock of the Enacims, in the Land of the children of Israel: saving the cities of Gaza, and Geth, and Azotus, in the which only they were left.

text.NRSV: So Joshua took the whole land, according to all that the Lord had spoken to Moses; and Joshua gave it for an inheritance to Israel according to their tribal allotments. And the land had rest from war.
text.Rotherham: So Joshua took the whole land, according to all that Yahweh had spoken unto Moses, and Joshua gave it for an inheritance unto Israel, according to their portions by their tribes,—and, the land, had rest from war.
text.KJV: So Joshua took the whole land, according to all that the Lord said unto Moses; and Joshua gave it for an inheritance unto Israel according to their divisions by their tribes. And the land rested from war.
text.NABRE: Thus Joshua took the whole land, just as the Lord had said to Moses. Joshua gave it to Israel as their heritage, apportioning it among the tribes. And the land had rest from war.
text.DRB: Josue therefore took all the Land, as our Lord spake to Moyses, and delivered it in possession to the children of Israel, according to their parts and tribes: and the Land rested from battles.

text.NRSV: Now these are the kings of the land, whom the Israelites defeated, whose land they occupied beyond the Jordan toward the east, from the Wadi Arnon to Mount Hermon, with all the Arabah eastward:
text.Rotherham: Now, these, are the kings of the land, whom the sons of Israel had smitten, and of whose land they had taken possession, over the Jordan, towards the rising of the sun,—from the ravine of Arnon, as far as Mount Hermon, and all the waste plain, on the east:
text.KJV: Now these are the kings of the land, which the children of Israel smote, and possessed their land on the other side Jordan toward the rising of the sun, from the river Arnon unto mount Hermon, and all the plain on the east:
text.NABRE: Chapter 12 Lists of Conquered Kings. These are the kings of the land whom the Israelites conquered and whose lands they occupied, east of the Jordan, from the River Arnon to Mount Hermon, including all the eastern section of the Arabah:
text.DRB: These are the kings, which the children of Israel struck, and possessed their Land beyond Jordan toward the rising of the sun, from the torrent Arnon unto mount Hermon, and all the East part, that looked toward the wilderness.

text.NRSV: King Sihon of the Amorites who lived at Heshbon, and ruled from Aroer, which is on the edge of the Wadi Arnon, and from the middle of the valley as far as the river Jabbok, the boundary of the Ammonites, that is, half of Gilead,
text.Rotherham: Sihon, king of the Amorites, who dwelt in Heshbon,—ruling from Aroer, which is on the edge of the ravine of Arnon, and the middle of the ravine, and half Gilead, even as far as the ravine Jabbok, the boundary of the sons of Ammon;
text.KJV: Sihon king of the Amorites, who dwelt in Heshbon, and ruled from Aroer, which is upon the bank of the river Arnon, and from the middle of the river, and from half Gilead, even unto the river Jabbok, which is the border of the children of Ammon;
text.NABRE: First, Sihon, king of the Amorites, who lived in Heshbon. His domain extended from Aroer, which is on the bank of the Wadi Arnon, to include the wadi itself, and the land northward through half of Gilead to the Wadi Jabbok at the border with the Ammonites,
text.DRB: Sehon the king of the Amorrheites, which dwelt in Hesebon, had dominion from Aroer, which is situated upon the bank of the torrent Arnon, & of the middle part in the valley, and of half Galaad, as far as the torrent Iaboc, which is the border of the children of Ammon:

text.NRSV: and the Arabah to the Sea of Chinneroth eastward, and in the direction of Beth-jeshimoth, to the sea of the Arabah, the Dead Sea, southward to the foot of the slopes of Pisgah;
text.Rotherham: and the waste plain as far as the sea of Chinneroth, on the east, even as far as the sea of the waste plain, the salt sea, on the east, the way to Bethjeshimoth,—and, on the south, under the slopes of Pisgah;
text.KJV: And from the plain to the sea of Chinneroth on the east, and unto the sea of the plain, even the salt sea on the east, the way to Beth–jeshimoth; and from the south, under Ashdoth–pisgah:
text.NABRE: as well as the Arabah from the eastern side of the Sea of Chinnereth, as far south as the eastern side of the Salt Sea of the Arabah in the direction of Beth-jeshimoth, southward under the slopes of Pisgah.
text.DRB: and from the wilderness, unto the sea of Ceneroth against the East, and unto the Sea of the wilderness, which is the most salt sea, on the East side by the way that leadeth to Besimoth, & on the South side, which lieth under Asedoth, as far as Phasga.

text.NRSV: and King Og of Bashan, one of the last of the Rephaim, who lived at Ashtaroth and at Edrei
text.Rotherham: and the boundary of Og, king of Bashan, of the remnant of the giants,—him who dwelt in Ashtaroth and in Edrei;
text.KJV: And the coast of Og king of Bashan, which was of the remnant of the giants, that dwelt at Ashtaroth and at Edrei,
text.NABRE: Secondly, the border of Og, king of Bashan, a survivor of the Rephaim, who lived at Ashtaroth and Edrei.
text.DRB: The border of Og the king of Basan, of the remnant of the Raphaims who dwelt in Astaroth, and in Edrai, and had dominion in mount Hermon, and in Salecha, and in all Basan, unto the borders

text.NRSV: and ruled over Mount Hermon and Salecah and all Bashan to the boundary of the Geshurites and the Maacathites, and over half of Gilead to the boundary of King Sihon of Heshbon.
text.Rotherham: ruling also in Mount Hermon, and in Salecah, and in all Bashan, as far as the boundary of the Geshurites, and the Maacathites,—and half Gilead, the boundary of Sihon king of Heshbon:—
text.KJV: And reigned in mount Hermon, and in Salcah, and in all Bashan, unto the border of the Geshurites and the Maachathites, and half Gilead, the border of Sihon king of Heshbon.
text.NABRE: He ruled over Mount Hermon, Salecah, and all Bashan as far as the boundary of the Geshurites and Maacathites, and over half of Gilead as far as the territory of Sihon, king of Heshbon.
text.DRB: of Gessuri, and Machati, and of the half part of Galaad: the borders of Sehon the king of Hesebon.

text.NRSV: Moses, the servant of the Lord , and the Israelites defeated them; and Moses the servant of the Lord gave their land for a possession to the Reubenites and the Gadites and the half-tribe of Manasseh.
text.Rotherham: Moses the servant of Yahweh, and the sons of Israel, had smitten them,—and Moses the servant of Yahweh, had given it, as a possession, unto the Reubenites, and unto the Gadites, and unto the half tribe of Manasseh.
text.KJV: Them did Moses the servant of the Lord and the children of Israel smite: and Moses the servant of the Lord gave it for a possession unto the Reubenites, and the Gadites, and the half tribe of Manasseh.
text.NABRE: It was Moses, the servant of the Lord, and the Israelites who conquered them; Moses, the servant of the Lord, gave possession of their land to the Reubenites, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh.
text.DRB: Moyses the servant of our Lord, and the children of Israel struck them, and Moyses delivered their Land in possession to the Rubenites, and Gadites, and the half tribe of Manasses.

text.NRSV: The following are the kings of the land whom Joshua and the Israelites defeated on the west side of the Jordan, from Baal-gad in the valley of Lebanon to Mount Halak, that rises toward Seir (and Joshua gave their land to the tribes of Israel as a possession according to their allotments,
text.Rotherham: And, these, are the kings of the land, whom Joshua and the sons of Israel smote over the Jordan, towards the west, from Baal-gad in the valley of the Lebanon, even as far as the Mount Halak that goeth up towards Seir,—and Joshua gave it unto the tribes of Israel, as a possession, according to their portions;
text.KJV: And these are the kings of the country which Joshua and the children of Israel smote on this side Jordan on the west, from Baal–gad in the valley of Lebanon even unto the mount Halak, that goeth up to Seir; which Joshua gave unto the tribes of Israel for a possession according to their divisions;
text.NABRE: This is a list of the kings of the land whom Joshua and the Israelites conquered west of the Jordan, from Baal-gad in the Lebanon valley to Mount Halak which rises toward Seir; Joshua apportioned their land and gave possession of it to the tribes of Israel;
text.DRB: These are the kings of the Land, whom Josue struck and the children of Israel beyond Jordan on the West side, from Balaalgad in the field of Libanus, unto the mount, part whereof goeth up into Seir: and Josue delivered it in possession to the tribes of Israel, to every one their portions.

text.NRSV: in the hill country, in the lowland, in the Arabah, in the slopes, in the wilderness, and in the Negeb, the land of the Hittites, Amorites, Canaanites, Perizzites, Hivites, and Jebusites):
text.Rotherham: in the hill country and in the lowland, and in the waste plain, and in the slopes, and in the desert, and in the south,—the Hittite, the Amorite, and the Canaanite, the Perizzite, the Hivite, and the Jebusite:—
text.KJV: In the mountains, and in the valleys, and in the plains, and in the springs, and in the wilderness, and in the south country; the Hittites, the Amorites, and the Canaanites, the Perizzites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites:
text.NABRE: it included the mountain regions and Shephelah, the Arabah, the slopes, the wilderness, and the Negeb, belonging to the Hittites, Amorites, Canaanites, Perizzites, Hivites, and Jebusites:
text.DRB: as well in the mountains as in the plain and champion countries. In Asedoth, and in the wilderness, and in the South was the Hetheite & the Amorrhite, the Chananite, and the Pherezite, the Heveite and the Jebusite.

text.Rotherham: The king of Jericho, one, the king of Ai, which is beside Bethel, one,
text.KJV: The king of Jericho, one; the king of Ai, which is beside Beth–el, one;
text.NABRE: The king of Jericho, one; the king of Ai, which is near Bethel, one;
text.DRB: The king of Jericho one: the king of Hai, which is on the side of Bethel, one:

text.Rotherham: The king of Jerusalem, one, the king of Hebron, one,
text.KJV: The king of Jerusalem, one; the king of Hebron, one;
text.NABRE: the king of Jerusalem, one; the king of Hebron, one;
text.DRB: the king of Jerusalem one, the king of Hebron one,

text.DRB: the king of Jerimoth one, the king of Lachis one,

text.NABRE: the king of Eglon, one; the king of Gezer, one;
text.DRB: the king of Eglon one, the king of Gazer one,

text.NABRE: the king of Debir, one; the king of Geder, one;
text.DRB: the king of Dabir one, the king of Gader one,

text.DRB: the king of Herma one, the king of Hered one,

text.NABRE: the king of Libnah, one; the king of Adullam, one;
text.DRB: the king of Lebna one, the king of Adullam one,

text.KJV: The king of Makkedah, one; the king of Beth–el, one;
text.NABRE: the king of Makkedah, one; the king of Bethel, one;
text.DRB: the king of Maceda one, the king of Bethel one,

text.KJV: The king of Tappuah, one; the king of Hepher, one;
text.NABRE: the king of Tappuah, one; the king of Hepher, one;
text.DRB: the king of Taphua one, the king of Opher one,

text.NRSV: the king of Aphek one the king of Lasharon one
text.Rotherham: The king of Aphek, one, the king of Lassharon, one,

text.NABRE: the king of Aphek, one; the king of Lasharon, one;
text.DRB: the king of Aphec one, the king of Saron one,

text.NABRE: the king of Madon, one; the king of Hazor, one;
text.DRB: the king of Madon one, the king of Asor one,

text.NABRE: the king of Shimron, one; the king of Achshaph, one;
text.DRB: the king of Semeron one, the king of Achsaph one,

text.NABRE: the king of Kedesh, one; the king of Jokneam, at Carmel, one;
text.DRB: the king of Cades one, the king of Iachanan one,

text.KJV: The king of Dor in the coast of Dor, one; the king of the nations of Gilgal, one;
text.NABRE: the king of Dor, in Naphath-dor, one; the king of Goyim at Gilgal, one;
text.DRB: the king of Carmel one, the king of Dor, and of the province of Dor one, the king of the Nations of Galgal one,

text.NRSV: the king of Tirzah one thirty-one kings in all.
text.Rotherham: The king of Tirzah, one,—All the kings, thirty and one.
text.KJV: The king of Tirzah, one: all the kings thirty and one.
text.NABRE: and the king of Tirzah, one—thirty-one kings in all.
text.DRB: the king of Thersa one: all the kings thirty one.

text.NRSV: Now Joshua was old and advanced in years; and the Lord said to him, “You are old and advanced in years, and very much of the land still remains to be possessed.
text.Rotherham: Now, Joshua, was old, advanced in days,—so then Yahweh said unto him—Thou, art old, advanced in days, and, much the larger part of the land, remaineth to be possessed.
text.KJV: Now Joshua was old and stricken in years; and the Lord said unto him, Thou art old and stricken in years, and there remaineth yet very much land to be possessed.
text.NABRE: II. Division of the Land Chapter 13 Division of Land Commanded. When Joshua was old and advanced in years, the Lord said to him: Though now you are old and advanced in years, a very large part of the land still remains to be possessed.
text.DRB: Josue was old, and striken in age, and our Lord said to him: Thou art old, and of a great age, and there is a very large country left, which is not yet divided by lot:

text.NRSV: This is the land that still remains: all the regions of the Philistines, and all those of the Geshurites
text.Rotherham: This, is the land that remaineth,—all the regions of the Philistines, and all the Geshuri;
text.KJV: This is the land that yet remaineth: all the borders of the Philistines, and all Geshuri,
text.NABRE: This is the remaining land: all Geshur and all the districts of the Philistines
text.DRB: to wit, all Galilee, Philisthiim, and all Gessuri.

text.NRSV: (from the Shihor, which is east of Egypt, northward to the boundary of Ekron, it is reckoned as Canaanite; there are five rulers of the Philistines, those of Gaza, Ashdod, Ashkelon, Gath, and Ekron), and those of the Avvim,
text.Rotherham: from the Shihor which faceth Egypt, even as far as the boundary of Ekron, northward, to the Canaanites, is it counted,—five princes of Philistines, the Gazites, and the Ashdodites, the Ashkalonites, the Gittites, and the Ekronites, also the Avvim
text.KJV: From Sihor, which is before Egypt, even unto the borders of Ekron northward, which is counted to the Canaanite: five lords of the Philistines; the Gazathites, and the Ashdothites, the Eshkalonites, the Gittites, and the Ekronites; also the Avites:
text.NABRE: (from the stream adjoining Egypt to the boundary of Ekron in the north is reckoned Canaanite territory, though held by the five lords of the Philistines in Gaza, Ashdod, Ashkelon, Gath, and Ekron);
text.DRB: From the troubled river, that watereth Ægypt, unto the borders of Accaron against the North: the Land of Chanaan, which is divided unto five lords of the Philisthiims, the Gazeites, the Azotians, the Ascalonites, the Getheites, and the Accaronites.

text.NRSV: in the south, all the land of the Canaanites, and Mearah that belongs to the Sidonians, to Aphek, to the boundary of the Amorites,
text.Rotherham: on the south: all the land of the Canaanites, and Mearah which belongeth to the Zidonians, as far as Aphek,—as far as the boundary of the Amorites;
text.KJV: From the south, all the land of the Canaanites, and Mearah that is beside the Sidonians, unto Aphek, to the borders of the Amorites:
text.NABRE: also where the Avvim are in the south; all the land of the Canaanites from Mearah of the Sidonians to Aphek, and the boundaries of the Amorites;
text.DRB: But on the South side are the Heveites, all the Land of Chanaan, and Maara of the Sidonians as far as Apheca, and the borders of the Amorrhite,

text.NRSV: and the land of the Gebalites, and all Lebanon, toward the east, from Baal-gad below Mount Hermon to Lebo-hamath,
text.Rotherham: and the land of the Gebalites, and all the Lebanon, to the rising of the sun, from Baal-gad, under Mount Hermon,—as far as the entering in of Hamath:
text.KJV: And the land of the Giblites, and all Lebanon, toward the sunrising, from Baal–gad under mount Hermon unto the entering into Hamath.
text.NABRE: and the Gebalite territory; and all the Lebanon on the east, from Baal-gad at the foot of Mount Hermon to Lebo-hamath.
text.DRB: and his confines. The country also of Libanus against the East from Baalgad under mount Hermon, till thou enter into Emath.

text.NRSV: all the inhabitants of the hill country from Lebanon to Misrephoth-maim, even all the Sidonians. I will myself drive them out from before the Israelites; only allot the land to Israel for an inheritance, as I have commanded you.
text.Rotherham: all the inhabitants of the hill country, from the Lebanon as far as Misrephoth-maim—all the Zidonians, I myself, will dispossess them from before the sons of Israel,—nevertheless, assign thou it by lot unto Israel, for an inheritance, as I have commanded thee.
text.KJV: All the inhabitants of the hill country from Lebanon unto Misrephoth–maim, and all the Sidonians, them will I drive out from before the children of Israel: only divide thou it by lot unto the Israelites for an inheritance, as I have commanded thee.
text.NABRE: All the inhabitants of the mountain regions between Lebanon and Misrephoth-maim, all Sidonians, I will drive out before the Israelites; at least include these areas in the division of the Israelite heritage, just as I have commanded you.
text.DRB: Of all that dwell in the mountain from Libanus, unto the Waters Maserephoth, and all the Sidonians. I am he that will destroy them from the face of the children of Israel. Let it come therefore into a portion of the inheritance of Israel, as I have commanded thee.

text.NRSV: Now therefore divide this land for an inheritance to the nine tribes and the half-tribe of Manasseh.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, apportion this land as an inheritance, unto the nine tribes,—and the half tribe of Manasseh.
text.KJV: Now therefore divide this land for an inheritance unto the nine tribes, and the half tribe of Manasseh,
text.NABRE: Now, therefore, apportion among the nine tribes and the half-tribe of Manasseh the land which is to be their heritage.
text.DRB: And now divide the Land in possession to the nine tribes, and to the half tribe of Manasses,

text.NRSV: With the other half-tribe of Manasseh the Reubenites and the Gadites received their inheritance, which Moses gave them, beyond the Jordan eastward, as Moses the servant of the Lord gave them:
text.Rotherham: With them, the, Reubenites and the Gadites, received their inheritance,—which Moses gave them beyond the Jordan, eastward, as Moses the servant of Yahweh gave unto them:
text.KJV: With whom the Reubenites and the Gadites have received their inheritance, which Moses gave them, beyond Jordan eastward, even as Moses the servant of the Lord gave them;
text.NABRE: The Eastern Tribes. Now the other half of the tribe of Manasseh, as well as the Reubenites and Gadites, had taken as their heritage what Moses, the servant of the Lord, had given them east of the Jordan:
text.DRB: with the which Ruben & Gad have possessed the Land, which Moyses the servant of our Lord delivered to them beyond the streams of Jordan, on the east side.

text.NRSV: from Aroer, which is on the edge of the Wadi Arnon, and the town that is in the middle of the valley, and all the tableland from Medeba as far as Dibon;
text.Rotherham: from Aroer, which is on the edge of the ravine of Arnon, and the city, which is in the midst of the ravine, and all the table-land of Medeba, as far as Dibon;
text.KJV: From Aroer, that is upon the bank of the river Arnon, and the city that is in the midst of the river, and all the plain of Medeba unto Dibon;
text.NABRE: from Aroer on the bank of the Wadi Arnon and the city in the wadi itself, through the tableland of Medeba and Dibon,
text.DRB: From Aroer, which is situate on the bank of the torrent Arnon, and in the midst of the valley, and all the champion of Medaba, as far as Dibon:

text.NRSV: and all the cities of King Sihon of the Amorites, who reigned in Heshbon, as far as the boundary of the Ammonites;
text.Rotherham: and all the cities of Sihon, king of the Amorites, who reigned in Heshbon,—as far as the boundary of the sons of Ammon;
text.KJV: And all the cities of Sihon king of the Amorites, which reigned in Heshbon, unto the border of the children of Ammon;
text.NABRE: with the rest of the cities of Sihon, king of the Amorites, who reigned in Heshbon, to the boundary of the Ammonites;
text.DRB: and all the cities of Cehon, the king of the Amorrhite, which reigned in Hesebon, unto the borders of the children of Ammon.

text.NRSV: and Gilead, and the region of the Geshurites and Maacathites, and all Mount Hermon, and all Bashan to Salecah;
text.Rotherham: and Gilead, and the boundary of the Geshurites and the Maacathites, and all Mount Hermon, and all Bashan, as far as Salecah;
text.KJV: And Gilead, and the border of the Geshurites and Maachathites, and all mount Hermon, and all Bashan unto Salcah;
text.NABRE: also Gilead and the territory of the Geshurites and Maacathites, all Mount Hermon, and all Bashan as far as Salecah,
text.DRB: And Galaad, and the border of Gessuri and Machati, and all mount Hermon, and all Basan, as far as Salecha,

text.NRSV: all the kingdom of Og in Bashan, who reigned in Ashtaroth and in Edrei (he alone was left of the survivors of the Rephaim); these Moses had defeated and driven out.
text.Rotherham: all the kingdom of Og, in Bashan, who reigned in Ashtaroth and in Edrei,—he, being left of the remnant of the giants; and Moses smote them and dispossessed them;
text.KJV: All the kingdom of Og in Bashan, which reigned in Ashtaroth and in Edrei, who remained of the remnant of the giants: for these did Moses smite, and cast them out.
text.NABRE: the entire kingdom in Bashan of Og, who was king at Ashtaroth and Edrei (he was a holdover from the remnant of the Rephaim). These Moses defeated and dispossessed.
text.DRB: all the Kingdom of Og in Basan, which reigned in Astaroth and Edrai, he was of the relics of the Raphaims: and Moyses struck, and destroyed them.

text.NRSV: Yet the Israelites did not drive out the Geshurites or the Maacathites; but Geshur and Maacath live within Israel to this day.
text.Rotherham: the sons of Israel, however, did not dispossess, the Geshurites or the Maacathites,—but Geshur and Maacath have remained in the midst of Israel until this day.
text.KJV: Nevertheless the children of Israel expelled not the Geshurites, nor the Maachathites: but the Geshurites and the Maachathites dwell among the Israelites until this day.
text.NABRE: But the Israelites did not dispossess the Geshurites and Maacathites, so that Geshur and Maacath dwell in the midst of Israel to this day.
text.DRB: And the children of Israel would not destroy Gessuri and Machati: and they have dwelt in the midst of Israel until this present day.

text.NRSV: To the tribe of Levi alone Moses gave no inheritance; the offerings by fire to the Lord God of Israel are their inheritance, as he said to them.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, unto the tribe of Levi, gave he no inheritance,—the altar-flames of Yahweh God of Israel, are his inheritance, as he spake unto him.
text.KJV: Only unto the tribe of Levi he gave none inheritance; the sacrifices of the Lord God of Israel made by fire are their inheritance, as he said unto them.
text.NABRE: However, Moses assigned no heritage to the tribe of Levi; the Lord, the God of Israel, is their heritage, as the Lord had promised them.
text.DRB: But to the tribe of Levi he gave no possession: but the sacrifices and victims of our Lord the God of Israel, that is his inheritance, as he spake to him.

text.NRSV: Moses gave an inheritance to the tribe of the Reubenites according to their clans.
text.Rotherham: Thus then Moses gave [an inheritance] unto the tribe of the sons of Reuben by their families;
text.KJV: And Moses gave unto the tribe of the children of Reuben inheritance according to their families.
text.NABRE: Reuben. This is what Moses gave to the tribe of the Reubenites by their clans:
text.DRB: Moyses therefore gave possession to the children of Ruben according to their kindreds.

text.NRSV: Their territory was from Aroer, which is on the edge of the Wadi Arnon, and the town that is in the middle of the valley, and all the tableland by Medeba;
text.Rotherham: and their boundary was from Aroer, that is on the edge of the ravine of Arnon and the city which is in the midst of the ravine and all the table-land by Medeba;
text.KJV: And their coast was from Aroer, that is on the bank of the river Arnon, and the city that is in the midst of the river, and all the plain by Medeba;
text.NABRE: Their territory reached from Aroer, on the bank of the Wadi Arnon, and the city in the wadi itself, through the tableland about Medeba,
text.DRB: And their border was from Aroer, which is situate on the bank of the torrent Arnon, and in the midst of the valley of the same torrent: all the plain, that leadeth to Medaba,

text.NRSV: with Heshbon, and all its towns that are in the tableland; Dibon, and Bamoth-baal, and Beth-baal-meon,
text.Rotherham: Heshbon and all her cities which are on the table-land,—Dibon and Bamoth-baal, and Beth-baal-meon;
text.KJV: Heshbon, and all her cities that are in the plain; Dibon, and Bamoth–baal, and Beth–baal–meon,
text.NABRE: to include Heshbon and all its towns on the tableland, Dibon, Bamoth-baal, Beth-baal-meon,
text.DRB: and Hesebon, and all their villages, which are in the champion. Dibon also, and Bamothbaal, and the town Baalmaon,

text.NRSV: and Jahaz, and Kedemoth, and Mephaath,
text.Rotherham: and Jahaz and Kedemoth and Maphaath;
text.KJV: And Jahazah, and Kedemoth, and Mephaath,
text.NABRE: Jahaz, Kedemoth, Mephaath,
text.DRB: and Jassa, and Cedimoth, and Mephaath,

text.NRSV: and Kiriathaim, and Sibmah, and Zereth-shahar on the hill of the valley,
text.Rotherham: and Kiriathaim and Sibmah, and Zareth-shahar on the mount of the vale;
text.KJV: And Kirjathaim, and Sibmah, and Zareth–shahar in the mount of the valley,
text.NABRE: Kiriathaim, Sibmah, Zereth-shahar on the knoll within the valley,
text.DRB: and Cariathaim, and Sabama, and Sarathasar in the mountain of the Valley.

text.NRSV: and Beth-peor, and the slopes of Pisgah, and Beth-jeshimoth,
text.Rotherham: and Beth-peor, and the slopes of Pisgah, and Beth-jeshimoth;
text.KJV: And Beth–peor, and Ashdoth–pisgah, and Beth–jeshimoth,
text.NABRE: Beth-peor, the slopes of Pisgah, Beth-jeshimoth,
text.DRB: Bethphogor and Asedoth, Phasga and Bethiesimoth,

text.NRSV: that is, all the towns of the tableland, and all the kingdom of King Sihon of the Amorites, who reigned in Heshbon, whom Moses defeated with the leaders of Midian, Evi and Rekem and Zur and Hur and Reba, as princes of Sihon, who lived in the land.
text.Rotherham: and all the cities of the table-land, and all the kingdom of Sihon king of the Amorites, who reigned in Heshbon,—whom Moses smote—with the princes of Midian, Evi and Rekem and Zur and Hur and Reba, dukes of Sihon dwelling in the land;
text.KJV: And all the cities of the plain, and all the kingdom of Sihon king of the Amorites, which reigned in Heshbon, whom Moses smote with the princes of Midian, Evi, and Rekem, and Zur, and Hur, and Reba, which were dukes of Sihon, dwelling in the country.
text.NABRE: and the other cities of the tableland and of the whole kingdom of Sihon. This Amorite king, who reigned in Heshbon, Moses had defeated, with the princes of Midian, vassals of Sihon who were settled in the land: Evi, Rekem, Zur, Hur, and Reba;
text.DRB: and all the champion cities, and all the Kingdoms of Sehon the king of the Amorrhite, that reigned in Hesebon, whom Moyses struck with the Princes of Madian: the Heveite, and Recem, and Sur, and Hur, and Rebe Dukes of Sehon inhabitants of the Land.

text.NRSV: Along with the rest of those they put to death, the Israelites also put to the sword Balaam son of Beor, who practiced divination.
text.Rotherham: Balaam also son of Beor, the diviner, did the sons of Israel slay with the sword among their slain.
text.KJV: Balaam also the son of Beor, the soothsayer, did the children of Israel slay with the sword among them that were slain by them.
text.NABRE: Balaam, son of Beor, the diviner, the Israelites killed with the sword, together with those they struck down.
text.DRB: And Balaam the son of Beor the soothsayer, did the children of Israel kill by the sword, with the rest that were slain.

text.NRSV: And the border of the Reubenites was the Jordan and its banks. This was the inheritance of the Reubenites, according to their families with their towns and villages.
text.Rotherham: And so the boundary of the sons of Reuben was the Jordan and district. This, is the inheritance of the sons of Reuben by their families, the cities and the villages thereof.
text.KJV: And the border of the children of Reuben was Jordan, and the border thereof. This was the inheritance of the children of Reuben after their families, the cities and the villages thereof.
text.NABRE: The boundary of the Reubenites was the Jordan. These cities and their villages were the heritage of the Reubenites by their clans.
text.DRB: And the river of Jordan was made the border of the children of Ruben. This is the possession of the Rubenites by their kindreds of cities and villages.

text.NRSV: Moses gave an inheritance also to the tribe of the Gadites, according to their families.
text.Rotherham: And Moses gave [an inheritance] unto the tribe of Gad, unto the sons of Gad by their families;
text.KJV: And Moses gave inheritance unto the tribe of Gad, even unto the children of Gad according to their families.
text.NABRE: Gad. This is what Moses gave to the tribe of the Gadites by their clans:
text.DRB: And Moyses gave to the tribe of Gad & to his children possession by their kindreds, the division whereof is this:

text.NRSV: Their territory was Jazer, and all the towns of Gilead, and half the land of the Ammonites, to Aroer, which is east of Rabbah,
text.Rotherham: and their boundary was—Jazer, and all the cities of Gilead, and half the land of the sons of Ammon,—as far as Aroer, which faceth Rabbah;
text.KJV: And their coast was Jazer, and all the cities of Gilead, and half the land of the children of Ammon, unto Aroer that is before Rabbah;
text.NABRE: Their territory included Jazer, all the cities of Gilead, and half the land of the Ammonites as far as Aroer, toward Rabbah
text.DRB: The border of Jaser, and all the cities of Galaad, and the half part of the Land of the children of Ammon: as far as Aroer, which is against Rabba:

text.NRSV: and from Heshbon to Ramath-mizpeh and Betonim, and from Mahanaim to the territory of Debir,
text.Rotherham: and from Heshbon as far as Ramath-mizpeh, and Botonim,—and from Mahanaim as far as the boundary of Debir;
text.KJV: And from Heshbon unto Ramath–mizpeh, and Betonim; and from Mahanaim unto the border of Debir;
text.NABRE: (that is, from Heshbon to Ramath-mizpeh and Betonim, and from Mahanaim to the boundary of Lo-debar);
text.DRB: and from Hesebon unto Ramoth, Masphe and Betonim: and from Manaim unto the borders of Dabir.

text.NRSV: and in the valley Beth-haram, Beth-nimrah, Succoth, and Zaphon, the rest of the kingdom of King Sihon of Heshbon, the Jordan and its banks, as far as the lower end of the Sea of Chinnereth, eastward beyond the Jordan.
text.Rotherham: and in the valley—Beth-haram and Beth-nimrah and Succoth and Zaphon, the rest of the kingdom of Sihon king of Heshbon, the Jordan and district,—as far as the end of the sea of Chinnereth, beyond the Jordan eastward.
text.KJV: And in the valley, Beth–aram, and Beth–nimrah, and Succoth, and Zaphon, the rest of the kingdom of Sihon king of Heshbon, Jordan and his border, even unto the edge of the sea of Chinnereth on the other side Jordan eastward.
text.NABRE: and in the Jordan valley: Beth-haram, Beth-nimrah, Succoth, Zaphon, the other part of the kingdom of Sihon, king of Heshbon, with the bank of the Jordan to the southeastern tip of the Sea of Chinnereth.
text.DRB: In the valley also Bethhara, and Bethnemra, and Socoth, and Saphon the other part of the Kingdom of Sehon the king of Hesebon: the end of this also is Jordan, unto the uttermost part of the sea Cenereth beyond Jordan on the east side.

text.NRSV: This is the inheritance of the Gadites according to their clans, with their towns and villages.
text.Rotherham: This, is the inheritance of the sons of Gad, by their families,—the cities and the villages thereof.
text.KJV: This is the inheritance of the children of Gad after their families, the cities, and their villages.
text.NABRE: These cities and their villages were the heritage of the clans of the Gadites.
text.DRB: This is the possession of the children of Gad by their families, their cities, and villages.

text.NRSV: Moses gave an inheritance to the half-tribe of Manasseh; it was allotted to the half-tribe of the Manassites according to their families.
text.Rotherham: And Moses gave [an inheritance] unto the half tribe of Manasseh,—so it belonged to the half tribe of the sons of Manasseh by their families;
text.KJV: And Moses gave inheritance unto the half tribe of Manasseh: and this was the possession of the half tribe of the children of Manasseh by their families.
text.NABRE: Manasseh. This is what Moses gave to the half-tribe of Manasseh; the half-tribe of the Manassites, by their clans, had
text.DRB: He gave also to the half tribe of Manasses and their children possession according to their kindreds,

text.NRSV: Their territory extended from Mahanaim, through all Bashan, the whole kingdom of King Og of Bashan, and all the settlements of Jair, which are in Bashan, sixty towns,
text.Rotherham: and their boundary was—from Mahanaim all Bashan all the kingdom of Og king of Bashan and all the Encampments of Jair which are in Bashan three-score cities;
text.KJV: And their coast was from Mahanaim, all Bashan, all the kingdom of Og king of Bashan, and all the towns of Jair, which are in Bashan, threescore cities:
text.NABRE: territory including Mahanaim, all of Bashan, the entire kingdom of Og, king of Bashan, and all the villages of Jair, which are sixty cities in Bashan.
text.DRB: the beginning whereof is this: From Manaim all Basan, and all the Kingdoms of Og the king of Basan, and all the villages of Jair, which are in Basan, threescore towns.

text.NRSV: and half of Gilead, and Ashtaroth, and Edrei, the towns of the kingdom of Og in Bashan; these were allotted to the people of Machir son of Manasseh according to their clans—for half the Machirites.
text.Rotherham: and, half Gilead and Ashtaroth and Edrei, cities of the kingdom of Og in Bashan, pertained to the sons of Machir son of Manasseh, to half the sons of Machir by their families.
text.KJV: And half Gilead, and Ashtaroth, and Edrei, cities of the kingdom of Og in Bashan, were pertaining unto the children of Machir the son of Manasseh, even to the one half of the children of Machir by their families.
text.NABRE: Half of Gilead, with Ashtaroth and Edrei, royal cities of Og in Bashan, fell to the descendants of Machir, son of Manasseh, to half the Machirites, by their clans.
text.DRB: And the half part of Galaad, and Astaroth, and Edrai, cities of the Kingdom of Og in Basan: to the children of Machir, the son of Manasses, to the half part of the children of Machir according to their kindreds.

text.NRSV: These are the inheritances that Moses distributed in the plains of Moab, beyond the Jordan east of Jericho.
text.Rotherham: These, are [the portions] which Moses gave for inheritance, in the waste plains of Moab,—over the Jordan by Jericho eastward.
text.KJV: These are the countries which Moses did distribute for inheritance in the plains of Moab, on the other side Jordan, by Jericho, eastward.
text.NABRE: These are the heritages which Moses gave when he was in the plains of Moab, beyond the Jordan east of Jericho.
text.DRB: This possession divided Moyses in the champion countries of Moab, beyond Jordan, against Jericho on the East side.

text.NRSV: But to the tribe of Levi Moses gave no inheritance; the Lord God of Israel is their inheritance, as he said to them.
text.Rotherham: But, unto the tribe of Levi, Moses gave no inheritance,—Yahweh God of Israel, he, was their inheritance, as he spake unto them.
text.KJV: But unto the tribe of Levi Moses gave not any inheritance: the Lord God of Israel was their inheritance, as he said unto them.
text.NABRE: But Moses gave no heritage to the tribe of Levi: the Lord, the God of Israel, is their heritage, as he had promised them.
text.DRB: But to the tribe of Levi he gave no possession: because our Lord the God of Israel himself is their possession, as he spake to them.

text.NRSV: These are the inheritances that the Israelites received in the land of Canaan, which the priest Eleazar, and Joshua son of Nun, and the heads of the families of the tribes of the Israelites distributed to them.
text.Rotherham: And, these, are the inheritances which the sons of Israel received in the land of Canaan,—which Eleazar the priest, and Joshua the son of Nun, and the ancestral heads of the tribes of the sons of Israel gave them to inherit.
text.KJV: And these are the countries which the children of Israel inherited in the land of Canaan, which Eleazar the priest, and Joshua the son of Nun, and the heads of the fathers of the tribes of the children of Israel, distributed for inheritance to them.
text.NABRE: Chapter 14 The Western Tribes. These are the portions which the Israelites received as heritage in the land of Canaan. Eleazar the priest, Joshua, son of Nun, and the heads of families in the tribes of the Israelites determined
text.DRB: This is it, which the children of Israel possessed in the Land of Chanaan, which Eleazar the Priest, and Josue the son of Nun, and the Princes of the families by the tribes of Israel gave to them:

text.NRSV: Their inheritance was by lot, as the Lord had commanded Moses for the nine and one-half tribes.
text.Rotherham: By lot, was their inheritance divided,—as Yahweh commanded by the hand of Moses, unto the nine tribes and unto the half tribe.
text.KJV: By lot was their inheritance, as the Lord commanded by the hand of Moses, for the nine tribes, and for the half tribe.
text.NABRE: their heritage by lot, as the Lord had commanded through Moses concerning the remaining nine and a half tribes.
text.DRB: dividing all things by lot, as our Lord had commanded in the hand of Moyses, to the nine tribes, and the half tribe.

text.NRSV: For Moses had given an inheritance to the two and one-half tribes beyond the Jordan; but to the Levites he gave no inheritance among them.
text.Rotherham: For Moses had given the two tribes and the half tribe their inheritance over the Jordan,—but, unto the Levites, gave he no inheritance in their midst;
text.KJV: For Moses had given the inheritance of two tribes and an half tribe on the other side Jordan: but unto the Levites he gave none inheritance among them.
text.NABRE: To two and a half tribes Moses had already given a heritage beyond the Jordan; to the Levites he had given no heritage among them:
text.DRB: For to two tribes and a half Moyses had given possession beyond Jordan: besides the Levites, which received no land among their brethren:

text.NRSV: For the people of Joseph were two tribes, Manasseh and Ephraim; and no portion was given to the Levites in the land, but only towns to live in, with their pasture lands for their flocks and herds.
text.Rotherham: for the sons of Joseph became two tribes: Manasseh and Ephraim,—so they gave no portion to the Levites in the land, save only cities to dwell in, with the pasture lands thereof, for their cattle and for their substance.
text.KJV: For the children of Joseph were two tribes, Manasseh and Ephraim: therefore they gave no part unto the Levites in the land, save cities to dwell in, with their suburbs for their cattle and for their substance.
text.NABRE: the descendants of Joseph formed two tribes, Manasseh and Ephraim. But the Levites were given no share of the land except cities to live in, with their pasture lands for the herds and flocks.
text.DRB: but into their place succeeded the children of Joseph divided into two tribes, of Manasses and Ephraim: neither did the Levites receive other portion in the Land, but cities to inhabit, and their suburbs to feed their beasts and cattle.

text.NRSV: The Israelites did as the Lord commanded Moses; they allotted the land.
text.Rotherham: As Yahweh commanded Moses, so, did the sons of Israel,—when they apportioned the land.
text.KJV: As the Lord commanded Moses, so the children of Israel did, and they divided the land.
text.NABRE: As the Lord had commanded Moses, so the Israelites did: they apportioned the land.
text.DRB: As our Lord had commanded Moyses, so did the children of Israel, and they divided the Land.

text.NRSV: Then the people of Judah came to Joshua at Gilgal; and Caleb son of Jephunneh the Kenizzite said to him, “You know what the Lord said to Moses the man of God in Kadesh-barnea concerning you and me.
text.Rotherham: Then came near the sons of Judah unto Joshua in Gilgal, and Caleb the son of Jephunneh, the Kenizzite, said unto him,—Thou, knowest the word which Yahweh spake unto Moses the man of God in my behalf and in thine in Kadesh-barnea.
text.KJV: Then the children of Judah came unto Joshua in Gilgal: and Caleb the son of Jephunneh the Kenezite said unto him, Thou knowest the thing that the Lord said unto Moses the man of God concerning me and thee in Kadesh–barnea.
text.NABRE: Caleb’s Portion. When the Judahites approached Joshua in Gilgal, the Kenizzite Caleb, son of Jephunneh, said to him: “You know the word the Lord spoke to Moses, the man of God, concerning you and concerning me in Kadesh-barnea.
text.DRB: Therefore the children of Juda came to Josue in Galgal, and Caleb the son of Jephone the Cenezeite spake to him: Thou knowest what our Lord spake to Moyses the man of God concerning me and thee in Cadesbarne.

text.NRSV: I was forty years old when Moses the servant of the Lord sent me from Kadesh-barnea to spy out the land; and I brought him an honest report.
text.Rotherham: Forty years old, was I when Moses the servant of Yahweh sent me from Kadesh-barnea to spy out the land,—so I brought him back word, as was in my heart.
text.KJV: Forty years old was I when Moses the servant of the Lord sent me from Kadesh–barnea to espy out the land; and I brought him word again as it was in mine heart.
text.NABRE: I was forty years old when Moses, the servant of the Lord, sent me from Kadesh-barnea to reconnoiter the land; and I brought back to him a frank report.
text.DRB: Forty years old was I when Moyses the servant of our Lord sent me from Cadesbarne, to view the Land, & I reported to him that which to me seemed true.

text.NRSV: But my companions who went up with me made the heart of the people melt; yet I wholeheartedly followed the Lord my God.
text.Rotherham: But, my brethren who had been up with me, made the heart of the people melt,—whereas, I, wholly followed Yahweh my God.
text.KJV: Nevertheless my brethren that went up with me made the heart of the people melt: but I wholly followed the Lord my God.
text.NABRE: My fellow scouts who went up with me made the people’s confidence melt away, but I was completely loyal to the Lord, my God.
text.DRB: But my brethren, that had gone up with me, discouraged the heart of the people: and I nevertheless followed our Lord God.

text.NRSV: And Moses swore on that day, saying, ‘Surely the land on which your foot has trodden shall be an inheritance for you and your children forever, because you have wholeheartedly followed the Lord my God.’
text.Rotherham: So then Moses sware on that day saying, Surely, the land on which thy foot hath trodden, unto thee, shall belong, for an inheritance, and unto thy sons, unto times age-abiding,—because thou hast wholly followed Yahweh my God.
text.KJV: And Moses sware on that day, saying, Surely the land whereon thy feet have trodden shall be thine inheritance, and thy children’s for ever, because thou hast wholly followed the Lord my God.
text.NABRE: On that occasion Moses swore this oath, ‘The land where you have set foot shall become your heritage and that of your descendants forever, because you have been completely loyal to the Lord, my God.’
text.DRB: And Moyses sware in that day, saying: The Land, which thy foot hath trodden, shall be thy possession, and thy children's for ever, because thou hast followed our Lord my God.

text.NRSV: And now, as you see, the Lord has kept me alive, as he said, these forty-five years since the time that the Lord spoke this word to Moses, while Israel was journeying through the wilderness; and here I am today, eighty-five years old.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore lo! Yahweh hath kept me alive as he spake these forty and five years from the time Yahweh spake this word unto Moses, while Israel journeyed in the desert. Now, therefore lo! I—to-day, am eighty and five years old:
text.KJV: And now, behold, the Lord hath kept me alive, as he said, these forty and five years, even since the Lord spake this word unto Moses, while the children of Israel wandered in the wilderness: and now, lo, I am this day fourscore and five years old.
text.NABRE: Now, as he promised, the Lord has preserved me these forty-five years since the Lord spoke thus to Moses while Israel journeyed in the wilderness; and now I am eighty-five years old,
text.DRB: Our Lord therefore hath granted me life, as he promised until this present day. It is forty and five years, since our Lord spake this word to Moyses, when Israel walked through the wilderness: this day am I eighty and five years old,

text.NRSV: I am still as strong today as I was on the day that Moses sent me; my strength now is as my strength was then, for war, and for going and coming.
text.Rotherham: I remain, to-day, as courageous as on the day when Moses sent me, as my strength was then, so also my strength is now,—for war, both to go out and to come in.
text.KJV: As yet I am as strong this day as I was in the day that Moses sent me: as my strength was then, even so is my strength now, for war, both to go out, and to come in.
text.NABRE: but I am still as strong today as I was the day Moses sent me forth, with no less vigor whether it be for war or for any other tasks.
text.DRB: so lusty, as I was at that time when I was sent to view: the strength of that time continueth in me until this day, as well to fight as to go.

text.NRSV: So now give me this hill country of which the Lord spoke on that day; for you heard on that day how the Anakim were there, with great fortified cities; it may be that the Lord will be with me, and I shall drive them out, as the Lord said.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, give me this mountain, whereof Yahweh spake, on that day,—for, thou thyself, didst hear, on that day, that, Anakim, were there, and great cities fortified, if so be Yahweh be with me, then shall I dispossess them, as spake Yahweh.
text.KJV: Now therefore give me this mountain, whereof the Lord spake in that day; for thou heardest in that day how the Anakims were there, and that the cities were great and fenced: if so be the Lord will be with me, then I shall be able to drive them out, as the Lord said.
text.NABRE: Now give me this mountain region which the Lord promised me that day, as you yourself heard. True, the Anakim are there, with large fortified cities, but if the Lord is with me I shall be able to dispossess them, as the Lord promised.”
text.DRB: Give me therefore this mountain, which our Lord promised, thyself also hearing it, wherein are the Enacims, and great cities and fenced: if perhaps our Lord be with me, and I shall be able to destroy them, as he promised me.

text.NRSV: Then Joshua blessed him, and gave Hebron to Caleb son of Jephunneh for an inheritance.
text.Rotherham: So Joshua blessed him, and gave Hebron unto Caleb son of Jephunneh, for an inheritance.
text.KJV: And Joshua blessed him, and gave unto Caleb the son of Jephunneh Hebron for an inheritance.
text.NABRE: Joshua blessed Caleb, son of Jephunneh, and gave him Hebron as his heritage.
text.DRB: And Josue blessed him, and delivered to him Hebron in possession.

text.NRSV: So Hebron became the inheritance of Caleb son of Jephunneh the Kenizzite to this day, because he wholeheartedly followed the Lord, the God of Israel.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, hath Hebron belonged unto Caleb son of Jephunneh the Kenezzite, for an inheritance, unto this day,—because he wholly followed Yahweh, God of Israel.
text.KJV: Hebron therefore became the inheritance of Caleb the son of Jephunneh the Kenezite unto this day, because that he wholly followed the Lord God of Israel.
text.NABRE: Therefore Hebron remains the heritage of the Kenizzite Caleb, son of Jephunneh, to the present day, because he was completely loyal to the Lord, the God of Israel.
text.DRB: And from thence-forth Hebron belonged to Caleb the son of Jephone the Cenezeite, until this present day: because he followed our Lord the God of Israel.

text.NRSV: Now the name of Hebron formerly was Kiriath-arba; this Arba was the greatest man among the Anakim. And the land had rest from war.
text.Rotherham: Now, the name of Hebron, was formerly, City of Arba, the greatest man among the Anakim, was he. And, the land, had rest from war.
text.KJV: And the name of Hebron before was Kirjath–arba; which Arba was a great man among the Anakims. And the land had rest from war.
text.NABRE: Hebron was formerly called Kiriath-arba, for Arba, the greatest among the Anakim. And the land had rest from war.
text.DRB: The name of Hebron before was called Cariath Arbe: Adam the greatest among the Enacims was laid there: and the Land ceased from battles.

text.NRSV: The lot for the tribe of the people of Judah according to their families reached southward to the boundary of Edom, to the wilderness of Zin at the farthest south.
text.Rotherham: So then the boundary of the tribe of the sons of Judah, by their families,—reached unto the boundary of Edom, the desert of Zin southward, on the extreme south;
text.KJV: This then was the lot of the tribe of the children of Judah by their families; even to the border of Edom the wilderness of Zin southward was the uttermost part of the south coast.
text.NABRE: Chapter 15 Boundaries of Judah. The lot for the tribe of Judah by their clans fell toward the boundary of Edom, the wilderness of Zin in the Negeb, in the extreme south.
text.DRB: Therefore the lot of the children of Judas by their kindreds was this: From the border of Edom, unto the desert of Sin against the South, and unto the uttermost part of the south coast.

text.NRSV: And their south boundary ran from the end of the Dead Sea, from the bay that faces southward;
text.Rotherham: so their south boundary beginneth at the end of the Salt Sea,—from the bay that turneth to the south;
text.KJV: And their south border was from the shore of the salt sea, from the bay that looketh southward:
text.NABRE: Their southern boundary ran from the end of the Salt Sea, from the tongue of land that faces the Negeb,
text.DRB: the beginning thereof was from the top of the most salt sea, and from the brink thereof, that looketh to the South.

text.NRSV: it goes out southward of the ascent of Akrabbim, passes along to Zin, and goes up south of Kadesh-barnea, along by Hezron, up to Addar, makes a turn to Karka,
text.Rotherham: and goeth forth on the south of the cliffs of Akrabbim, and crosseth over to Zin, and goeth up on the south of Kadesh-barnea,—and passeth over by Hezron, and goeth up to Addar, and turneth round to Karka;
text.KJV: And it went out to the south side to Maaleh–acrabbim, and passed along to Zin, and ascended up on the south side unto Kadesh–barnea, and passed along to Hezron, and went up to Adar, and fetched a compass to Karkaa:
text.NABRE: and went southward below the pass of Akrabbim, across through Zin, up to a point south of Kadesh-barnea, across to Hezron, and up to Addar; from there, looping around Karka,
text.DRB: And it goeth forth against the Ascent of the Scorpion, & passeth through into Sina: and riseth up into Cadesbarne, and reacheth into Esron, ascending to Addar, and compassing Carcaa,

text.NRSV: passes along to Azmon, goes out by the Wadi of Egypt, and comes to its end at the sea. This shall be your south boundary.
text.Rotherham: and passeth over to Azmon, and goeth forth at the ravine of Egypt, and so the extensions of the boundary are to the sea. This, shall be your south boundary.
text.KJV: From thence it passed toward Azmon, and went out unto the river of Egypt; and the goings out of that coast were at the sea: this shall be your south coast.
text.NABRE: it crossed to Azmon and then joined the Wadi of Egypt before coming out at the sea. (This is your southern boundary.)
text.DRB: and thence passing through into Asemona, & reaching to the Torrent of Ægypt: and the borders thereof shall be the great sea. This shall be the end of the south coast.

text.NRSV: And the east boundary is the Dead Sea, to the mouth of the Jordan. And the boundary on the north side runs from the bay of the sea at the mouth of the Jordan;
text.Rotherham: And, a boundary eastward, is the Salt Sea, as far as the end of the Jordan. And, the boundary on the north side, is from the bay of the sea, from the end of the Jordan.
text.KJV: And the east border was the salt sea, even unto the end of Jordan. And their border in the north quarter was from the bay of the sea at the uttermost part of Jordan:
text.NABRE: The eastern boundary was the Salt Sea as far as the mouth of the Jordan. The northern boundary climbed northward from the tongue of the sea, toward the mouth of the Jordan,
text.DRB: But on the East side the beginning shall be the most salt sea unto the utmost parts of Jordan: & those places that look to the North from the brink of the sea unto the same river of Jordan.

text.NRSV: and the boundary goes up to Beth-hoglah, and passes along north of Beth-arabah; and the boundary goes up to the Stone of Bohan, Reuben’s son;
text.Rotherham: And the boundary goeth up Beth-hoglah, and passeth over, on the north, by Beth-arabah,—and the boundary goeth up by the Stone of Bohan, son of Reuben;
text.KJV: And the border went up to Beth–hogla, and passed along by the north of Beth–arabah; and the border went up to the stone of Bohan the son of Reuben:
text.NABRE: up to Beth-hoglah, and ran north of Beth-arabah, up to Eben-Bohan-ben-Reuben.
text.DRB: And the border goeth up into Beth Hagla, and passeth from the north into Beth Araba: ascending to the stone of Bohen the son of Ruben.

text.NRSV: and the boundary goes up to Debir from the Valley of Achor, and so northward, turning toward Gilgal, which is opposite the ascent of Adummim, which is on the south side of the valley; and the boundary passes along to the waters of En-shemesh, and ends at En-rogel;
text.Rotherham: and the boundary goeth up towards Debir, out of the vale of Achor, then northward, turning unto Gilgal, which is over against the ascent of Adummim, which is on the south side of the ravine,—then the boundary passeth over unto the waters of En-shemesh, and so the extensions thereof are unto En-rogel:
text.KJV: And the border went up toward Debir from the valley of Achor, and so northward, looking toward Gilgal, that is before the going up to Adummim, which is on the south side of the river: and the border passed toward the waters of En–shemesh, and the goings out thereof were at En–rogel:
text.NABRE: Thence the boundary climbed to Debir, north of the Valley of Achor, in the direction of the Gilgal that faces the pass of Adummim, on the south side of the wadi; from there it crossed to the waters of En-shemesh and emerged at En-rogel.
text.DRB: And reaching as far as the borders of Debera from the Valley of Achor, against the North looking toward Galgal, which is opposite to the Ascent of Adommim, on the south side of the torrent: and passeth the waters, that are called The Fountain of the sun: and the ends thereof shall be to the Fountain Rogel.

text.NRSV: then the boundary goes up by the valley of the son of Hinnom at the southern slope of the Jebusites (that is, Jerusalem); and the boundary goes up to the top of the mountain that lies over against the valley of Hinnom, on the west, at the northern end of the valley of Rephaim;
text.Rotherham: then ascendeth the boundary by the valley of the son of Hinnom, to the side of the Jebusite, on the south, the same, is Jerusalem,—and the boundary goeth up unto the top of the mountain which faceth the valley of Hinnom, to the west, which is at the end of the Vale of Giants, northwards;
text.KJV: And the border went up by the valley of the son of Hinnom unto the south side of the Jebusite; the same is Jerusalem: and the border went up to the top of the mountain that lieth before the valley of Hinnom westward, which is at the end of the valley of the giants northward:
text.NABRE: Climbing again to the Valley of Ben-hinnom on the southern flank of the Jebusites (that is, Jerusalem), the boundary rose to the top of the mountain at the northern end of the Valley of Rephaim, which bounds the Valley of Hinnom on the west.
text.DRB: And it ascendeth by the Valley of the son of Ennom on the side of the Jebusite toward the South, this is Jerusalem: and thence rearing it-self to the top of the mountain, which is against Geennom toward the West in the top of the Valley of Raphaim against the North.

text.NRSV: then the boundary extends from the top of the mountain to the spring of the Waters of Nephtoah, and from there to the towns of Mount Ephron; then the boundary bends around to Baalah (that is, Kiriath-jearim);
text.Rotherham: and the boundary turneth round, from the top of the mountain, unto the fountain of the waters of Nephtoah, and goeth out unto the cities of Mount Ephron,—then the boundary turneth round to Baalah, the same, is Kiriath-jearim;
text.KJV: And the border was drawn from the top of the hill unto the fountain of the water of Nephtoah, and went out to the cities of mount Ephron; and the border was drawn to Baalah, which is Kirjath–jearim:
text.NABRE: From the top of the mountain it ran to the fountain of waters of Nephtoah, extended to the cities of Mount Ephron, and continued to Baalah, or Kiriath-jearim.
text.DRB: And it passeth through from the top of the mountain to the fountain of the water Nephtoa: and reacheth to the towns of mount Ephron: and bendeth into Baala, which is Cariathiarim, that is to say, a city of woods.

text.NRSV: and the boundary circles west of Baalah to Mount Seir, passes along to the northern slope of Mount Jearim (that is, Chesalon), and goes down to Beth-shemesh, and passes along by Timnah;
text.Rotherham: then the boundary goeth round from Baalah westward, unto Mount Seir, and passeth over unto the slope of Mount Yearim, on the north, the same, is Chesalon,—and descendeth Bethshemesh, and passeth over Timnah;
text.KJV: And the border compassed from Baalah westward unto mount Seir, and passed along unto the side of mount Jearim, which is Chesalon, on the north side, and went down to Beth–shemesh, and passed on to Timnah:
text.NABRE: From Baalah the boundary curved westward to Mount Seir and passed north of the ridge of Mount Jearim (that is, Chesalon); it descended to Beth-shemesh, and ran across to Timnah.
text.DRB: And it compasseth from Baala against the West, unto mount Seir: and passeth by the side of mount Iarim toward the North into Cheslon: and goeth down into Bethsames, and passeth into Thamna.

text.NRSV: the boundary goes out to the slope of the hill north of Ekron, then the boundary bends around to Shikkeron, and passes along to Mount Baalah, and goes out to Jabneel; then the boundary comes to an end at the sea.
text.Rotherham: then the boundary goeth forth unto the side of Ekron, northward, then the boundary turneth round towards Shikkeron, and crosseth over Mount Baalah, and goeth out at Jabneel,—and the extensions of the boundary are to the sea.
text.KJV: And the border went out unto the side of Ekron northward: and the border was drawn to Shicron, and passed along to mount Baalah, and went out unto Jabneel; and the goings out of the border were at the sea.
text.NABRE: It then extended along the northern flank of Ekron, continued through Shikkeron, and across to Mount Baalah, from there to include Jabneel, before it came out at the sea.
text.DRB: And it reacheth toward the North coast of a part of Accaron at the side: and bendeth to Sechrona, and passeth mount Baala: and cometh into Iebneel, and is shut up with the end of the great sea toward the West.

text.NRSV: And the west boundary was the Mediterranean with its coast. This is the boundary surrounding the people of Judah according to their families.
text.Rotherham: And, as a west boundary, are the great sea and coast. This, is the boundary of the sons of Judah, round about, by their families.
text.KJV: And the west border was to the great sea, and the coast thereof. This is the coast of the children of Judah round about according to their families.
text.NABRE: The western boundary was the Great Sea and its coast. This was the complete boundary of the Judahites by their clans.
text.DRB: These are the borders of the children of Judas in circuit in their kindreds.

text.NRSV: According to the commandment of the Lord to Joshua, he gave to Caleb son of Jephunneh a portion among the people of Judah, Kiriath-arba, that is, Hebron (Arba was the father of Anak).
text.Rotherham: And, to Caleb, son of Jephunneh, gave he a portion, in the midst of the sons of Judah, at the bidding of Yahweh, by Joshua,—even the City of Arba, father of Anak, the same, is Hebron.
text.KJV: And unto Caleb the son of Jephunneh he gave a part among the children of Judah, according to the commandment of the Lord to Joshua, even the city of Arba the father of Anak, which city is Hebron.
text.NABRE: Conquest by Caleb. As the Lord had commanded, Joshua gave Caleb, son of Jephunneh, a portion among the Judahites, namely, Kiriath-arba (Arba was the father of Anak), that is, Hebron.
text.DRB: But to Caleb the son of Jephone he gave a portion in the midst of the children of Judas, as our Lord had commanded him: Cariath Arbe the father of Enac, that is Hebron.

text.NRSV: And Caleb drove out from there the three sons of Anak: Sheshai, Ahiman, and Talmai, the descendants of Anak.
text.Rotherham: And Caleb dispossessed from thence, the three sons of Anak,—Sheshai, and Ahiman, and Talmai, the descendants of Anak.
text.KJV: And Caleb drove thence the three sons of Anak, Sheshai, and Ahiman, and Talmai, the children of Anak.
text.NABRE: And Caleb dispossessed from there the three Anakim, the descendants of Anak: Sheshai, Ahiman, and Talmai.
text.DRB: And Caleb destroyed out of it the three sons of Enac, Sesai and Ahiman and Tholmai of the stock of Enac.

text.NRSV: From there he went up against the inhabitants of Debir; now the name of Debir formerly was Kiriath-sepher.
text.Rotherham: And he went up from thence, against the inhabitants of Debir,—now, the name of Debir, formerly, was Kiriath-sepher.
text.KJV: And he went up thence to the inhabitants of Debir: and the name of Debir before was Kirjath–sepher.
text.NABRE: From there he marched up against the inhabitants of Debir, which was formerly called Kiriath-sepher.
text.DRB: And from thence going up he came to the inhabitants of Dabir, which before was called Cariath Sepher, that is to say, a city of letters.

text.NRSV: And Caleb said, “Whoever attacks Kiriath-sepher and takes it, to him I will give my daughter Achsah as wife.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Caleb, He that smiteth Kiriath-sepher, and captureth it, I will give unto him Achsah, my daughter, to wife.
text.KJV: And Caleb said, He that smiteth Kirjath–sepher, and taketh it, to him will I give Achsah my daughter to wife.
text.NABRE: Caleb said, “To the man who attacks Kiriath-sepher and captures it, I will give my daughter Achsah in marriage.”
text.DRB: And Caleb said: He that shall strike Cariath-sepher, and take it, I will give him Axa my daughter to wife.

text.NRSV: Othniel son of Kenaz, the brother of Caleb, took it; and he gave him his daughter Achsah as wife.
text.Rotherham: And Othniel son of Kenaz, brother of Caleb, captured it,—so he gave unto him Achsah, his daughter, to wife.
text.KJV: And Othniel the son of Kenaz, the brother of Caleb, took it: and he gave him Achsah his daughter to wife.
text.NABRE: Othniel captured it, the son of Caleb’s brother Kenaz; so Caleb gave him his daughter Achsah in marriage.
text.DRB: And Othoniel the son of Cenez, the younger brother of Caleb took it: & he gave him Axa his daughter to wife.

text.NRSV: When she came to him, she urged him to ask her father for a field. As she dismounted from her donkey, Caleb said to her, “What do you wish?”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when she came, that she moved him to ask of her father a field, and, when she alighted from off the ass, Caleb said unto her—What aileth thee?
text.KJV: And it came to pass, as she came unto him, that she moved him to ask of her father a field: and she lighted off her ass; and Caleb said unto her, What wouldest thou?
text.NABRE: When she came to him, she induced him to ask her father for some land. Then, as she alighted from the donkey, Caleb asked her, “What do you want?”
text.DRB: Who going together, she was moved by her husband to ask a field of her father, and she sighed as she sat on her ass. To whom Caleb said: What aileth thee?

text.NRSV: She said to him, “Give me a present; since you have set me in the land of the Negeb, give me springs of water as well.” So Caleb gave her the upper springs and the lower springs.
text.Rotherham: And she said—Give me a present, for, dry land, hast thou given me, therefore must thou give me, pools of water. So he gave her upper pools and lower pools.
text.KJV: Who answered, Give me a blessing; for thou hast given me a south land; give me also springs of water. And he gave her the upper springs, and the nether springs.
text.NABRE: She answered, “Give me a present! Since you have assigned to me land in the Negeb, give me also pools of water.” So he gave her the upper and the lower pools. Cities of Judah.
text.DRB: But she answered: Give me a blessing: A South and dry Land thou hast given me, join also a watery. Caleb therefore gave her a watery ground above & beneath.

text.NRSV: This is the inheritance of the tribe of the people of Judah according to their families.
text.Rotherham: This, is the inheritance of the tribe of the sons of Judah, by their families.
text.KJV: This is the inheritance of the tribe of the children of Judah according to their families.
text.NABRE: This is the heritage of the tribe of Judahites by their clans:
text.DRB: This is the possession of the tribe of the children of Judas by their kindreds.

text.NRSV: The towns belonging to the tribe of the people of Judah in the extreme South, toward the boundary of Edom, were Kabzeel, Eder, Jagur,
text.Rotherham: And the uttermost cities of the tribe of the sons of Judah, towards the boundary of Edom in the south, were,—Kabzeel and Eder, and Jagur,
text.KJV: And the uttermost cities of the tribe of the children of Judah toward the coast of Edom southward were Kabzeel, and Eder, and Jagur,
text.NABRE: The cities of the tribe of the Judahites in the extreme southern district toward Edom were: Kabzeel, Eder, Jagur,
text.DRB: And the cities from the uttermost parts of the children of Judas by the borders of Edom on the South were Cabseel and Eder and Iagur,

text.NRSV: Kinah, Dimonah, Adadah,
text.Rotherham: and Kinah and Dimonah, and Adadah,—
text.KJV: And Kinah, and Dimonah, and Adadah,
text.NABRE: Kinah, Dimonah, Adadah,
text.DRB: & Cina and Dimona and Adada,

text.NRSV: Kedesh, Hazor, Ithnan,
text.Rotherham: and Kedesh and Hazor, and Ithnan,
text.KJV: And Kedesh, and Hazor, and Ithnan,
text.NABRE: Kedesh, Hazor, and Ithnan;
text.DRB: and Cades, and Asor, and Iethnan,

text.NRSV: Ziph, Telem, Bealoth,
text.Rotherham: Ziph and Talem, and Bealoth,
text.KJV: Ziph, and Telem, and Bealoth,
text.NABRE: Ziph, Telem, Bealoth,
text.DRB: Ziph and Telem and Baloth,

text.NRSV: Hazor-hadattah, Kerioth-hezron (that is, Hazor),
text.Rotherham: and Hazor-hadattah and Kerioth-hezron, the same, is Hazor;
text.KJV: And Hazor, Hadattah, and Kerioth, and Hezron, which is Hazor,
text.NABRE: Hazor-hadattah, and Kerioth-hezron (that is, Hazor);
text.DRB: Asor the new and Carioth, Hesron, this is Asor.

text.NRSV: Amam, Shema, Moladah,
text.Rotherham: Amam and Shema, and Moladah,
text.KJV: Amam, and Shema, and Moladah,
text.NABRE: Amam, Shema, Moladah,
text.DRB: Amam, Sama, and Molada,

text.NRSV: Hazar-gaddah, Heshmon, Beth-pelet,
text.Rotherham: and Hazar-gaddah and Heshmon, and Beth-pelet.
text.KJV: And Hazar–gaddah, and Heshmon, and Beth–palet,
text.NABRE: Hazar-gaddah, Heshmon, Beth-pelet,
text.DRB: and Asergadda and Hassemon and Bethphelet,

text.NRSV: Hazar-shual, Beer-sheba, Biziothiah,
text.Rotherham: and Hazarshual and Beer-sheba, with the hamlets thereof,
text.KJV: And Hazar–shual, and Beer–sheba, and Bizjothjah,
text.NABRE: Hazar-shual, Beer-sheba, and Biziothiah;
text.DRB: and Hasersual and Bersabee and Baziothia

text.NRSV: Baalah, Iim, Ezem,
text.Rotherham: Baalah and Iyyim, and Ezem,
text.KJV: Baalah, and Iim, and Azem,
text.NABRE: Baalah, Iim, Ezem,
text.DRB: and Baala and Jim & Esem,

text.NRSV: Eltolad, Chesil, Hormah,
text.Rotherham: and Eltolad and Chesil, and Hormah,
text.KJV: And Eltolad, and Chesil, and Hormah,
text.NABRE: Eltolad, Chesil, Hormah,
text.DRB: and Eltholad and Cesil and Harma

text.NRSV: Ziklag, Madmannah, Sansannah,
text.Rotherham: and Ziklag and Madmannah, and Sansannah,
text.KJV: And Ziklag, and Madmannah, and Sansannah,
text.NABRE: Ziklag, Madmannah, Sansannah,
text.DRB: and Siceleg, and Medemena and Sensenna,

text.NRSV: Lebaoth, Shilhim, Ain, and Rimmon: in all, twenty-nine towns, with their villages.
text.Rotherham: and Lebaoth and Shilhim, and En-rimmon,—all the cities are twenty-nine, with their villages.
text.KJV: And Lebaoth, and Shilhim, and Ain, and Rimmon: all the cities are twenty and nine, with their villages:
text.NABRE: Lebaoth, Shilhim, and Ain and Rimmon; a total of twenty-nine cities with their villages.
text.DRB: Labaoth and Selim & Æn and Remon. All the cities twenty nine, and their villages.

text.NRSV: And in the Lowland, Eshtaol, Zorah, Ashnah,
text.Rotherham: In the lowland,—Eshtaol and Zorah, and Ashnah,
text.KJV: And in the valley, Eshtaol, and Zoreah, and Ashnah,
text.NABRE: In the Shephelah: Eshtaol, Zorah, Ashnah,
text.DRB: But in the champion countries: Estaol and Sarea and Asena,

text.NRSV: Zanoah, En-gannim, Tappuah, Enam,
text.Rotherham: and Zanoah and En-gannim, Tappuah, and Enam;
text.KJV: And Zanoah, and En–gannim, Tappuah, and Enam,
text.NABRE: Zanoah, Engannim, Tappuah, Enam,
text.DRB: and Sanoe and Engannim and Taphua and Enaim,

text.NRSV: Jarmuth, Adullam, Socoh, Azekah,
text.Rotherham: Jarmuth, and Adullam, Socoh, and Azekah;
text.KJV: Jarmuth, and Adullam, Socoh, and Azekah,
text.NABRE: Jarmuth, Adullam, Socoh, Azekah,
text.DRB: and Jerimoth, and Adullam, Socho & Azeca,

text.NRSV: Shaaraim, Adithaim, Gederah, Gederothaim: fourteen towns with their villages.
text.Rotherham: and Shaaraim, and Adithaim, and Gederah, and Gederothaim,—fourteen cities, with their villages.
text.KJV: And Sharaim, and Adithaim, and Gederah, and Gederothaim; fourteen cities with their villages:
text.NABRE: Shaaraim, Adithaim, Gederah, and Gederothaim; fourteen cities and their villages.
text.DRB: & Saraim and Adithaim and Gedera and Gederothaim: fourteen cities, and their villages.

text.NRSV: Zenan, Hadashah, Migdal-gad,
text.Rotherham: Zenan and Hadashah, and Migdal-gad,
text.KJV: Zenan, and Hadashah, and Migdal–gad,
text.NABRE: Zenan, Hadashah, Migdal-gad,
text.DRB: Sanan and Hadassa and Magdalgad,

text.NRSV: Dilan, Mizpeh, Jokthe-el,
text.Rotherham: and Dilan and Mizpeh, and Joktheel,
text.KJV: And Dilean, and Mizpeh, and Joktheel,
text.NABRE: Dilean, Mizpeh, Joktheel,
text.DRB: Delean and Masepha and Iecthel,

text.NRSV: Lachish, Bozkath, Eglon,
text.Rotherham: Lachish and Bozkath, and Eglon,
text.KJV: Lachish, and Bozkath, and Eglon,
text.NABRE: Lachish, Bozkath, Eglon,
text.DRB: Lachis and Bascath and Eglon,

text.NRSV: Cabbon, Lahmam, Chitlish,
text.Rotherham: and Cabbon and Lahmas, and Chithlish,
text.KJV: And Cabbon, and Lahmam, and Kithlish,
text.NABRE: Cabbon, Lahmas, Chitlish,
text.DRB: Chebbon and Leheman and Cethlis

text.NRSV: Gederoth, Beth-dagon, Naamah, and Makkedah: sixteen towns with their villages.
text.Rotherham: and Gederoth, Beth-dagon and Naamah, and Makkedah,—sixteen cities, with their villages.
text.KJV: And Gederoth, Beth–dagon, and Naamah, and Makkedah; sixteen cities with their villages:
text.NABRE: Gederoth, Beth-dagon, Naamah, and Makkedah; sixteen cities and their villages.
text.DRB: and Gideroth and Bethdagon and Naama & Maceda: sixteen cities, and their villages.

text.NRSV: Libnah, Ether, Ashan,
text.Rotherham: Libnah and Ether, and Ashan,
text.KJV: Libnah, and Ether, and Ashan,
text.NABRE: Libnah, Ether, Ashan,
text.DRB: Labana and Ether and Asan,

text.NRSV: Iphtah, Ashnah, Nezib,
text.Rotherham: and Iphtah and Ashnah, and Nezib,
text.KJV: And Jiphtah, and Ashnah, and Nezib,
text.NABRE: Iphtah, Ashnah, Nezib,
text.DRB: Iephtha and Esna and Nesib,

text.NRSV: Keilah, Achzib, and Mareshah: nine towns with their villages.
text.Rotherham: and Keilah and Achzib, and Mareshah,—nine cities, with their villages.
text.KJV: And Keilah, and Achzib, and Mareshah; nine cities with their villages:
text.NABRE: Keilah, Achzib, and Mareshah; nine cities and their villages.
text.DRB: and Ceila and Achzib and Mareza: nine cities, and their villages.

text.NRSV: Ekron, with its dependencies and its villages;
text.Rotherham: Ekron with her towns, and her villages.
text.KJV: Ekron, with her towns and her villages:
text.NABRE: Ekron and its towns and villages;
text.DRB: Accaron with the towns and villages thereof.

text.NRSV: from Ekron to the sea, all that were near Ashdod, with their villages.
text.Rotherham: From Ekron, even unto the sea,—all that were by the side of Ashdod with their villages.
text.KJV: From Ekron even unto the sea, all that lay near Ashdod, with their villages:
text.NABRE: from Ekron to the sea, all the towns that lie alongside Ashdod, and their villages;
text.DRB: From Accaron unto the sea: all places that bend toward Azotus & the villages thereof.

text.NRSV: Ashdod, its towns and its villages; Gaza, its towns and its villages; to the Wadi of Egypt, and the Great Sea with its coast.
text.Rotherham: Ashdod, her towns and her villages. Gaza, her towns and her villages, as far as the ravine of Egypt,—and the great sea and coast.
text.KJV: Ashdod with her towns and her villages, Gaza with her towns and her villages, unto the river of Egypt, and the great sea, and the border thereof:
text.NABRE: Ashdod and its towns and villages; Gaza and its towns and villages, as far as the Wadi of Egypt and the coast of the Great Sea.
text.DRB: Azotus with the towns & villages thereof. Gaza with the towns & villages thereof, unto the torrent of Ægypt, & the great sea is the border thereof.

text.NRSV: And in the hill country, Shamir, Jattir, Socoh,
text.Rotherham: And in the hill-country,—Shamir and Jattir, and Socoh,
text.KJV: And in the mountains, Shamir, and Jattir, and Socoh,
text.NABRE: In the mountain regions: Shamir, Jattir, Socoh,
text.DRB: And in the mountain: Samir & Jether & Socoth

text.NRSV: Dannah, Kiriath-sannah (that is, Debir),
text.Rotherham: and Dannah and Kiriath-sannah, the same, is Debir,
text.KJV: And Dannah, and Kirjath–sannah, which is Debir,
text.NABRE: Dannah, Kiriath-sannah (that is, Debir),
text.DRB: & Danna & Cariath senna, this is Dabir:

text.NRSV: Anab, Eshtemoh, Anim,
text.Rotherham: and Anab and Eshtemoh, and Anim,
text.KJV: And Anab, and Eshtemoh, and Anim,
text.NABRE: Anab, Eshtemoh, Anim,
text.DRB: Anab & Istemo & Anim,

text.NRSV: Goshen, Holon, and Giloh: eleven towns with their villages.
text.Rotherham: and Goshen and Holon, and Giloh,—eleven cities, with their villages.
text.KJV: And Goshen, and Holon, and Giloh; eleven cities with their villages:
text.NABRE: Goshen, Holon, and Giloh; eleven cities and their villages.
text.DRB: Gosen & Olon & Gilo: eleven cities & their villages.

text.NRSV: Arab, Dumah, Eshan,
text.Rotherham: Arab and Rumah and Eshan,
text.KJV: Arab, and Dumah, and Eshean,
text.NABRE: Arab, Dumah, Eshan,
text.DRB: Arab & Ruma & Esaan,

text.NRSV: Janim, Beth-tappuah, Aphekah,
text.Rotherham: and Janim and Beth-tappuah and Aphekah,
text.KJV: And Janum, and Beth–tappuah, and Aphekah,
text.NABRE: Janim, Beth-tappuah, Aphekah,
text.DRB: & Ianum & Beth thaphua & Apheca,

text.NRSV: Humtah, Kiriath-arba (that is, Hebron), and Zior: nine towns with their villages.
text.Rotherham: and Humtah, and Kiriath-arba, the same, is Hebron, and Zior,—nine cities, with their villages.
text.KJV: And Humtah, and Kirjath–arba, which is Hebron, and Zior; nine cities with their villages:
text.NABRE: Humtah, Kiriath-arba (that is, Hebron), and Zior; nine cities and their villages.
text.DRB: Athmatha and Cariath-arbe, this is Hebron, and Sior: nine cities and their villages.

text.NRSV: Maon, Carmel, Ziph, Juttah,
text.Rotherham: Maon, Carmel, and Ziph and Jutah,
