text.KJV: Maon, Carmel, and Ziph, and Juttah,
text.NABRE: Maon, Carmel, Ziph, Juttah,
text.DRB: Maon and Carmel and Ziph and Iota,

text.NRSV: Jezreel, Jokdeam, Zanoah,
text.Rotherham: and Jezreel and Jokdeam, and Zanoah,
text.KJV: And Jezreel, and Jokdeam, and Zanoah,
text.NABRE: Jezreel, Jokdeam, Zanoah,
text.DRB: Jezrael & Iucadam and Zanoe,

text.NRSV: Kain, Gibeah, and Timnah: ten towns with their villages.
text.Rotherham: Kain, Gibeah and Timnah,—ten cities, with their villages.
text.KJV: Cain, Gibeah, and Timnah; ten cities with their villages:
text.NABRE: Kain, Gibbeah, and Timnah; ten cities and their villages.
text.DRB: Accain, Gabaa and Thamna: ten cities and their villages.

text.NRSV: Halhul, Beth-zur, Gedor,
text.Rotherham: Halhul, Beth-zur, and Gedor,
text.KJV: Halhul, Beth–zur, and Gedor,
text.NABRE: Halhul, Beth-zur, Gedor,
text.DRB: Halhul, and Bethsur, and Gedor,

text.NRSV: Maarath, Beth-anoth, and Eltekon: six towns with their villages.
text.Rotherham: and Maarath and Beth-anoth, and Eltekon,—six cities, with their villages.
text.KJV: And Maarath, and Beth–anoth, and Eltekon; six cities with their villages:
text.NABRE: Maarath, Beth-anoth, and Eltekon; six cities and their villages. Tekoa, Ephrathah (that is, Bethlehem), Peor, Etam, Kulom, Tatam, Zores, Karim, Gallim, Bether, and Manoko; eleven cities and their villages.
text.DRB: Mareth, and Beth-anoth, and Eltecon: six cities and their villages.

text.NRSV: Kiriath-baal (that is, Kiriath-jearim), and Rabbah: two towns with their villages.
text.Rotherham: Kiriath-baal, the same, is Kiriath-jearim, and Rabbah,—two cities, with their villages.
text.KJV: Kirjath–baal, which is Kirjath–jearim, and Rabbah; two cities with their villages:
text.NABRE: Kiriath-baal (that is, Kiriath-jearim) and Rabbah; two cities and their villages.
text.DRB: Cariathbaal, this is Cariathiarim the city of woods, and Arebba: two cities and their villages.

text.NRSV: In the wilderness, Beth-arabah, Middin, Secacah,
text.Rotherham: In the desert,—Beth-arabah, Middin, and Secacah,
text.KJV: In the wilderness, Beth–arabah, Middin, and Secacah,
text.NABRE: In the wilderness: Beth-arabah, Middin, Secacah,
text.DRB: In the desert Beth-araba, Meddin, and Sachacha,

text.NRSV: Nibshan, the City of Salt, and En-gedi: six towns with their villages.
text.Rotherham: and Nibshan and the City of Salt, and En-gedi,—six cities, with their villages.
text.KJV: And Nibshan, and the city of Salt, and En–gedi; six cities with their villages.
text.NABRE: Nibshan, Ir-hamelah, and En-gedi; six cities and their villages.
text.DRB: and Nebsan, and the city of salt, and Engaddi: six cities, and their villages.

text.NRSV: But the people of Judah could not drive out the Jebusites, the inhabitants of Jerusalem; so the Jebusites live with the people of Judah in Jerusalem to this day.
text.Rotherham: But, as for the Jebusites, the inhabitants of Jerusalem, the sons of Judah could not dispossess them,—so the Jebusites have dwelt with the sons of Judah, in Jerusalem, until this day.
text.KJV: As for the Jebusites the inhabitants of Jerusalem, the children of Judah could not drive them out: but the Jebusites dwell with the children of Judah at Jerusalem unto this day.
text.NABRE: But the Jebusites who lived in Jerusalem the Judahites could not dispossess; so the Jebusites dwell in Jerusalem beside the Judahites to the present day.
text.DRB: But the Jebusite the inhabitant of Jerusalem the children of Judas could not destroy: and the Jebusite dwelt with the children of Judas in Jerusalem until this present day.

text.NRSV: The allotment of the Josephites went from the Jordan by Jericho, east of the waters of Jericho, into the wilderness, going up from Jericho into the hill country to Bethel;
text.Rotherham: Then came out the lot, for the sons of Joseph, from the Jordan by Jericho, at the waters of Jericho, eastward,—the desert, going up from Jericho, through the hill country to Bethel;
text.KJV: And the lot of the children of Joseph fell from Jordan by Jericho, unto the water of Jericho on the east, to the wilderness that goeth up from Jericho throughout mount Beth–el,
text.NABRE: Chapter 16 The Joseph Tribes. The lot that fell to the Josephites extended from the Jordan at Jericho to the waters of Jericho east of the wilderness; then the boundary went up from Jericho to the heights at Bethel.
text.DRB: The lot also of the children of Joseph fell from Jordan against Jericho and the Waters thereof, on the east: the wilderness which goeth up from Jericho to the mountain of Bethel:

text.NRSV: then going from Bethel to Luz, it passes along to Ataroth, the territory of the Archites;
text.Rotherham: and it goeth out from Bethel towards Luz,—and passeth over unto the boundary of the Archites at Ataroth;
text.KJV: And goeth out from Beth–el to Luz, and passeth along unto the borders of Archi to Ataroth,
text.NABRE: Leaving Bethel for Luz, it crossed the ridge to the border of the Archites at Ataroth,
text.DRB: and goeth out from Bethel to Luza: and passeth the border of Archia, to Atharoth.

text.NRSV: then it goes down westward to the territory of the Japhletites, as far as the territory of Lower Beth-horon, then to Gezer, and it ends at the sea.
text.Rotherham: and descendeth westward, unto the boundary of the Japhletites, as far as the boundary of Bethhoron the nether, and as far as Gezer,—and the extensions thereof are to the sea.
text.KJV: And goeth down westward to the coast of Japhleti, unto the coast of Beth–horon the nether, and to Gezer: and the goings out thereof are at the sea.
text.NABRE: and descended westward to the border of the Japhletites, to that of the Lower Beth-horon, and to Gezer, and from there to the sea.
text.DRB: And descendeth Westward, by the border of Iephleti, unto the borders of Beth horon the lower, and to Gazer: and their countries are ended by the great sea:

text.NRSV: The Josephites—Manasseh and Ephraim—received their inheritance.
text.Rotherham: So the sons of Joseph, Manasseh and Ephraim, received their inheritance.
text.KJV: So the children of Joseph, Manasseh and Ephraim, took their inheritance.
text.NABRE: Ephraim. Within the heritage of Manasseh and Ephraim, sons of Joseph,
text.DRB: and Manasses and Ephraim the children of Joseph possessed it.

text.NRSV: The territory of the Ephraimites by their families was as follows: the boundary of their inheritance on the east was Ataroth-addar as far as Upper Beth-horon,
text.Rotherham: And the boundary of the sons of Ephraim, by their families, was,—yea the boundary of their inheritance on the east, was Ataroth-addar, as far as Beth-horon the upper;
text.KJV: And the border of the children of Ephraim according to their families was thus: even the border of their inheritance on the east side was Ataroth–addar, unto Beth–horon the upper;
text.NABRE: the dividing line for the heritage of the Ephraimites by their clans ran from east of Ataroth-addar to Upper Beth-horon
text.DRB: And the border of the children of Ephraim was made according to their kindreds: and their possession toward the East was Ataroth addar unto Beth-horon the higher.

text.NRSV: and the boundary goes from there to the sea; on the north is Michmethath; then on the east the boundary makes a turn toward Taanath-shiloh, and passes along beyond it on the east to Janoah,
text.Rotherham: and the boundary goeth out westward at Michmethath, on the north, then the boundary bendeth round easward, to Taanath-shiloh,—and passeth by it on the east to Janoah;
text.KJV: And the border went out toward the sea to Michmethah on the north side; and the border went about eastward unto Taanath–shiloh, and passed by it on the east to Janohah;
text.NABRE: and thence to the sea. From Michmethath on the north, their boundary curved eastward around Taanath-shiloh, and continued east of it to Janoah;
text.DRB: And the confines go out unto the sea: but Machmethath looketh to the North, and it compasseth the borders against the East into Thanath-selo: and passeth through on the East side to Janoe.

text.NRSV: then it goes down from Janoah to Ataroth and to Naarah, and touches Jericho, ending at the Jordan.
text.Rotherham: and goeth down from Janoah to Ataroth and to Naarah,—and toucheth upon Jericho, and goeth out at the Jordan.
text.KJV: And it went down from Janohah to Ataroth, and to Naarath, and came to Jericho, and went out at Jordan.
text.NABRE: from there it descended to Ataroth and Naarah, and reaching Jericho, it ended at the Jordan.
text.DRB: and it goeth down from Janoe into Ataroth and Naaratha: and it cometh into Jericho, & goeth out to Jordan.

text.NRSV: From Tappuah the boundary goes westward to the Wadi Kanah, and ends at the sea. Such is the inheritance of the tribe of the Ephraimites by their families,
text.Rotherham: From Tappuah, runneth the boundary along westward, to the ravine of Kanah, and the extensions thereof are to the sea,—this, is the inheritance of the tribe of the sons of Ephraim, by their families;
text.KJV: The border went out from Tappuah westward unto the river Kanah; and the goings out thereof were at the sea. This is the inheritance of the tribe of the children of Ephraim by their families.
text.NABRE: From Tappuah the boundary ran westward to the Wadi Kanah and ended at the sea. This was the heritage of the Ephraimites by their clans,
text.DRB: From Taphua it passeth through against the sea into the Valley of reeds, and the issues thereof are into the most salt sea. This is the possession of the tribe of the children of Ephraim by their families.

text.NRSV: together with the towns that were set apart for the Ephraimites within the inheritance of the Manassites, all those towns with their villages.
text.Rotherham: together with the cities which were separated for the sons of Ephraim, in the midst of the inheritance of the sons of Manasseh,—all the cities, with their villages.
text.KJV: And the separate cities for the children of Ephraim were among the inheritance of the children of Manasseh, all the cities with their villages.
text.NABRE: including the villages that belonged to each city set aside for the Ephraimites within the heritage of the Manassites.
text.DRB: And cities with their villages were separated to the children of Ephraim in the midst of the possession of the children of Manasses,

text.NRSV: They did not, however, drive out the Canaanites who lived in Gezer: so the Canaanites have lived within Ephraim to this day but have been made to do forced labor.
text.Rotherham: But they did not dispossess the Canaanites who were dwelling in Gazer,—so the Canaanites have dwelt in the midst of Ephraim unto this day, and have become tributary servants.
text.KJV: And they drave not out the Canaanites that dwelt in Gezer: but the Canaanites dwell among the Ephraimites unto this day, and serve under tribute.
text.NABRE: But they did not dispossess the Canaanites living in Gezer; they live within Ephraim to the present day, though they have been put to forced labor.
text.DRB: and the children of Ephraim slew not the Chananite, which dwelt in Gazer: and the Chananite dwelt in the midst of Ephraim until this day tributary.

text.NRSV: Then allotment was made to the tribe of Manasseh, for he was the firstborn of Joseph. To Machir the firstborn of Manasseh, the father of Gilead, were allotted Gilead and Bashan, because he was a warrior.
text.Rotherham: And the lot for the tribe of Manasseh was, for, he, was the firstborn of Joseph,—as for Machir, the firstborn of Manasseh, the father of Gilead, because, he, was a man of war, therefore had he Gilead and Bashan.
text.KJV: There was also a lot for the tribe of Manasseh; for he was the firstborn of Joseph; to wit, for Machir the firstborn of Manasseh, the father of Gilead: because he was a man of war, therefore he had Gilead and Bashan.
text.NABRE: Chapter 17 Manasseh. Now as for the lot that fell to the tribe of Manasseh as the firstborn of Joseph: since Manasseh’s eldest son, Machir, the father of Gilead, was a warrior, who had already obtained Gilead and Bashan,
text.DRB: And this lot fell to the tribe of Manasses (for he is the first born of Joseph:) To Machir the first born of Manasses the father of Galaad, who was a warlike man, and had for possession Galaad and Basan:

text.NRSV: And allotments were made to the rest of the tribe of Manasseh, by their families, Abiezer, Helek, Asriel, Shechem, Hepher, and Shemida; these were the male descendants of Manasseh son of Joseph, by their families.
text.Rotherham: So the lot pertained to the rest of the sons of Manasseh, by their families, to the sons of Abiezer, and to the sons of Helek, and to the sons of Asriel, and to the sons of Shechem, and to the sons of Hepher, and to the sons of Shemida,—these, were the sons of Manasseh, son of Joseph, even the male descendants, by their families.
text.KJV: There was also a lot for the rest of the children of Manasseh by their families; for the children of Abiezer, and for the children of Helek, and for the children of Asriel, and for the children of Shechem, and for the children of Hepher, and for the children of Shemida: these were the male children of Manasseh the son of Joseph by their families.
text.NABRE: the allotment was now made to the rest of the Manassites by their clans: the descendants of Abiezer, Helek, Asriel, Shechem, Hepher, and Shemida; these are the other male children of Manasseh, son of Joseph, by their clans.
text.DRB: and to the rest of the children of Manasses according to their families, to the children of Abiezer, & to the children of Helec, & to the children of Esriel, & to the children of Sechem, & to the children of Hepher, and to the children of Semida. These are the children of Manasses the son of Joseph, males by their kindreds.

text.NRSV: Now Zelophehad son of Hepher son of Gilead son of Machir son of Manasseh had no sons, but only daughters; and these are the names of his daughters: Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah.
text.Rotherham: But, Zelophehad, son of Hepher, son of Gilead, son of Machir, son of Manasseh, had no sons, but only daughters,—and, these, are the names of his daughters, Mahlah and Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah.
text.KJV: But Zelophehad, the son of Hepher, the son of Gilead, the son of Machir, the son of Manasseh, had no sons, but daughters: and these are the names of his daughters, Mahlah, and Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah.
text.NABRE: Furthermore, Zelophehad, son of Hepher, son of Gilead, son of Machir, son of Manasseh, had no sons, but only daughters, whose names were Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah.
text.DRB: But Salphaad the son of Hepher the son of Galaad the son of Machir the son of Manasses had no sons, but only daughters: whose names be these, Maala & Noa and Hegla and Melcha and Thersa.

text.NRSV: They came before the priest Eleazar and Joshua son of Nun and the leaders, and said, “The Lord commanded Moses to give us an inheritance along with our male kin.” So according to the commandment of the Lord he gave them an inheritance among the kinsmen of their father.
text.Rotherham: And they had come near, before Eleazar the priest, and before Joshua son of Nun, and before the princes, saying, Yahweh, commanded Moses, to give us an inheritance in the midst of our brethren,—And he had given them, according to the bidding of Yahweh, an inheritance, in the midst of the brethren of their father.
text.KJV: And they came near before Eleazar the priest, and before Joshua the son of Nun, and before the princes, saying, The Lord commanded Moses to give us an inheritance among our brethren. Therefore according to the commandment of the Lord he gave them an inheritance among the brethren of their father.
text.NABRE: These presented themselves to Eleazar the priest, to Joshua, son of Nun, and to the leaders, saying, “The Lord commanded Moses to give us a heritage among our relatives.” So in accordance with the command of the Lord a heritage was given them among their father’s relatives.
text.DRB: And they came in the presence of Eleazar the Priest, and of Josue the son of Nun, and of the Princes, saying: Our Lord commanded by the hand of Moyses, that a possession should be given us in the midst of our brethren. And he gave them according to the commandment of our Lord a possession in the midst of their father's brethren.

text.NRSV: Thus there fell to Manasseh ten portions, besides the land of Gilead and Bashan, which is on the other side of the Jordan,
text.Rotherham: Thus there fell ten portions to Manasseh,—besides the land of Gilead, and Bashan, which is beyond the Jordan;
text.KJV: And there fell ten portions to Manasseh, beside the land of Gilead and Bashan, which were on the other side Jordan;
text.NABRE: Thus ten shares fell to Manasseh apart from the land of Gilead and Bashan beyond the Jordan,
text.DRB: And the cords fell to Manasses, ten, beside the Land of Galaad and Basan beyond Jordan.

text.NRSV: because the daughters of Manasseh received an inheritance along with his sons. The land of Gilead was allotted to the rest of the Manassites.
text.Rotherham: because, the daughters of Manasseh, received an inheritance in the midst of his sons,—and, the land of Gilead, became the possession of the sons of Manasseh that remained.
text.KJV: Because the daughters of Manasseh had an inheritance among his sons: and the rest of Manasseh’s sons had the land of Gilead.
text.NABRE: since these female descendants of Manasseh received each a portion among his sons. The land of Gilead fell to the rest of the Manassites.
text.DRB: For the daughters of Manasses possessed inheritance in the midst of his sons. And the Land of Galaad fell to the lot of the children of Manasses that remained.

text.NRSV: The territory of Manasseh reached from Asher to Michmethath, which is east of Shechem; then the boundary goes along southward to the inhabitants of En-tappuah.
text.Rotherham: So then the boundary of Manasseh was from Asher, Michmethath, which faceth Shechem,—and the boundary goeth along unto the right hand, unto the inhabitants of En-tappuah.
text.KJV: And the coast of Manasseh was from Asher to Michmethah, that lieth before Shechem; and the border went along on the right hand unto the inhabitants of En–tappuah.
text.NABRE: Manasseh bordered on Asher. From Michmethath, near Shechem, another boundary ran southward to include the inhabitants of En-Tappuah,
text.DRB: And the border of Manasses from Aser, was Machmethath which looketh to Sichem: and goeth out on the right hand beside the inhabitants of the Fountain of Taphua.

text.NRSV: The land of Tappuah belonged to Manasseh, but the town of Tappuah on the boundary of Manasseh belonged to the Ephraimites.
text.Rotherham: Manasseh, had the land of Tappuah,—but, Tappuah itself, towards the boundary of Manasseh, pertained unto the sons of Ephraim;
text.KJV: Now Manasseh had the land of Tappuah: but Tappuah on the border of Manasseh belonged to the children of Ephraim;
text.NABRE: because the district of Tappuah belonged to Manasseh, although Tappuah itself was an Ephraimite city on the border of Manasseh.
text.DRB: For in the lot of Manasses was fallen the Land of Taphua, which is beside the borders of Manasses, the childrens of Ephraim.

text.NRSV: Then the boundary went down to the Wadi Kanah. The towns here, to the south of the wadi, among the towns of Manasseh, belong to Ephraim. Then the boundary of Manasseh goes along the north side of the wadi and ends at the sea.
text.Rotherham: and the boundary goeth down to the ravine of Kanah, southward of the ravine, these cities, belong to Ephraim, in the midst of the cities of Manasseh,—but, the boundary of Manasseh, was on the north side of the ravine, and the extensions thereof were to the sea;
text.KJV: And the coast descended unto the river Kanah, southward of the river: these cities of Ephraim are among the cities of Manasseh: the coast of Manasseh also was on the north side of the river, and the outgoings of it were at the sea:
text.NABRE: This same boundary continued down to the Wadi Kanah. The cities that belonged to Ephraim from among the cities in Manasseh were those to the south of that wadi; thus the territory of Manasseh ran north of the wadi and ended at the sea.
text.DRB: And the border of the Reed valley went down into the south of the torrent of the cities of Ephraim, which are in the midst of the cities of Manasses: the border of Manasses on the North of the torrent, and the issue thereof goeth to the sea:

text.NRSV: The land to the south is Ephraim’s and that to the north is Manasseh’s, with the sea forming its boundary; on the north Asher is reached, and on the east Issachar.
text.Rotherham: southward, pertaineth to Ephraim, and, northward, to Manasseh, and the sea was the boundary thereof,—and they touch, Asher, on the north, and Issachar on the east.
text.KJV: Southward it was Ephraim’s, and northward it was Manasseh’s, and the sea is his border; and they met together in Asher on the north, and in Issachar on the east.
text.NABRE: The land on the south belonged to Ephraim and that on the north to Manasseh; with the sea as their common boundary, they reached Asher on the north and Issachar on the east.
text.DRB: so that the possession of Ephraim is on the south, and on the North of Manasses, and the sea encloseth both, and they be joined one to another in the tribe of Aser on the North, and in the tribe of Issachar on the East.

text.NRSV: Within Issachar and Asher, Manasseh had Beth-shean and its villages, Ibleam and its villages, the inhabitants of Dor and its villages, the inhabitants of En-dor and its villages, the inhabitants of Taanach and its villages, and the inhabitants of Megiddo and its villages (the third is Naphath).
text.Rotherham: And Manasseh had—in Issachar and in Asher—Bethshean and her towns, and Ibleam and her towns, and the inhabitants of Dor and her towns, and the inhabitants of En-dor and her towns, and the inhabitants of Taanach and her towns, and the inhabitants of Megiddo, and her towns—the three heights,
text.KJV: And Manasseh had in Issachar and in Asher Beth–shean and her towns, and Ibleam and her towns, and the inhabitants of Dor and her towns, and the inhabitants of Endor and her towns, and the inhabitants of Taanach and her towns, and the inhabitants of Megiddo and her towns, even three countries.
text.NABRE: Moreover, in Issachar and in Asher Manasseh was awarded Beth-shean and its towns, Ibleam and its towns, the inhabitants of Dor and its towns, the inhabitants of Endor and its towns, the inhabitants of Taanach and its towns, the inhabitants of Megiddo and its towns (the third is Naphath-dor).
text.DRB: And the inheritance of Manasses in Issachar and in Aser was Bethsan and the villages thereof, and Jeblaam with the villages thereof, and the inhabitants of Dor with the towns thereof, the inhabitants also of Endor with the towns thereof, and in like manner the inhabitants of Thenac with the towns thereof, and the inhabitants of Mageddo with the towns thereof, and the third part of the city of Nopheth.

text.NRSV: Yet the Manassites could not take possession of those towns; but the Canaanites continued to live in that land.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Manasseh, however, could not dispossess these cities,—but the Canaanites were determined to remain in this land;
text.KJV: Yet the children of Manasseh could not drive out the inhabitants of those cities; but the Canaanites would dwell in that land.
text.NABRE: Since the Manassites were not able to dispossess these cities, the Canaanites continued to inhabit this region.
text.DRB: Neither could the children of Manasses overthrow the cities, but the Chananite began to dwell in his Land.

text.NRSV: But when the Israelites grew strong, they put the Canaanites to forced labor, but did not utterly drive them out.
text.Rotherham: but, when the sons of Israel had waxed strong, they put the Canaanites under tribute,—though they, dispossessed, them not.
text.KJV: Yet it came to pass, when the children of Israel were waxen strong, that they put the Canaanites to tribute; but did not utterly drive them out.
text.NABRE: When the Israelites grew stronger they put the Canaanites to forced labor, but they did not dispossess them.
text.DRB: But after that the children of Israel grew to be strong, they subdued the Chananites, and made them their tributaries, neither did they kill them.

text.NRSV: The tribe of Joseph spoke to Joshua, saying, “Why have you given me but one lot and one portion as an inheritance, since we are a numerous people, whom all along the Lord has blessed?”
text.Rotherham: Then spake the sons of Joseph unto Joshua, saying,—Why hast thou given me, as an inheritance, but one lot and one portion, seeing that, I, am a numerous people, because hitherto hath Yahweh blessed me.
text.KJV: And the children of Joseph spake unto Joshua, saying, Why hast thou given me but one lot and one portion to inherit, seeing I am a great people, forasmuch as the Lord hath blessed me hitherto?
text.NABRE: Protest of Joseph Tribes. The descendants of Joseph said to Joshua, “Why have you given us only one lot and one share as our heritage? Our people are too many, because of the extent to which the Lord has blessed us.”
text.DRB: And the children of Joseph spake to Josue, and said: Why hast thou given me the possession of one lot and cord, whereas I am of so great a multitude, & our Lord hath blessed me?

text.NRSV: And Joshua said to them, “If you are a numerous people, go up to the forest, and clear ground there for yourselves in the land of the Perizzites and the Rephaim, since the hill country of Ephraim is too narrow for you.”
text.Rotherham: And Joshua said unto them: If, a numerous people, thou art, get thee up to the forest, and cut down for thyself there, in the land of the Perizzites and of the Rephaim,—seeing that, too narrow for thee, is the hill country of Ephraim.
text.KJV: And Joshua answered them, If thou be a great people, then get thee up to the wood country, and cut down for thyself there in the land of the Perizzites and of the giants, if mount Ephraim be too narrow for thee.
text.NABRE: Joshua answered them, “If you are too many, go up to the forest and clear out a place for yourselves there in the land of the Perizzites and Rephaim, since the mountain regions of Ephraim are so narrow.”
text.DRB: To whom Josue said: If thou be a great people, go up into the wood, and cut thee room in the Land of the Pherezite and Raphaims: because the possession of mount Ephraim is narrow for thee.

text.NRSV: The tribe of Joseph said, “The hill country is not enough for us; yet all the Canaanites who live in the plain have chariots of iron, both those in Beth-shean and its villages and those in the Valley of Jezreel.”
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Joseph said, The hill country is not enough for us,—and there are, chariots of iron, among all the Canaanites that dwell in the land of the valley, belonging both to them in Bethshean, and her towns, and to them in the valley of Jezreel.
text.KJV: And the children of Joseph said, The hill is not enough for us: and all the Canaanites that dwell in the land of the valley have chariots of iron, both they who are of Beth–shean and her towns, and they who are of the valley of Jezreel.
text.NABRE: For the Josephites said, “Our mountain regions are not enough for us; on the other hand, the Canaanites living in the valley region all have iron chariots, in particular those in Beth-shean and its towns, and those in the valley of Jezreel.”
text.DRB: To whom the children of Joseph answered: We cannot go up to the mountains, whereas the Chananites that dwell in the champion country, wherein are situated Bethsan with the towns thereof and Jezrael possessing the midst of the valley, use iron chariots.

text.NRSV: Then Joshua said to the house of Joseph, to Ephraim and Manasseh, “You are indeed a numerous people, and have great power; you shall not have one lot only,
text.Rotherham: And Joshua made answer unto the house of Joseph, to Ephraim and to Manasseh, saying,—A numerous people, thou art, and, great vigour, thou hast, thou shalt not have one lot only;
text.KJV: And Joshua spake unto the house of Joseph, even to Ephraim and to Manasseh, saying, Thou art a great people, and hast great power: thou shalt not have one lot only:
text.NABRE: Joshua therefore said to Ephraim and Manasseh, the house of Joseph, “You are a numerous people and very strong. You shall not have merely one share,
text.DRB: And Josue said to the house of Joseph, of Ephraim, and Manasses: Thou art a great people, and of great strength, thou shalt not have one lot,

text.NRSV: but the hill country shall be yours, for though it is a forest, you shall clear it and possess it to its farthest borders; for you shall drive out the Canaanites, though they have chariots of iron, and though they are strong.”
text.Rotherham: for, the hill country, shall be thine, in that, a forest, it is, therefore canst thou cut it down, and thine shall be the extensions thereof,—for thou shalt dispossess the Canaanites, though, chariots of iron, they have, and though, strong, they are.
text.KJV: But the mountain shall be thine; for it is a wood, and thou shalt cut it down: and the outgoings of it shall be thine: for thou shalt drive out the Canaanites, though they have iron chariots, and though they be strong.
text.NABRE: for the mountain region which is now forest shall be yours when you clear it. Its adjacent land shall also be yours if, despite their strength and iron chariots, you dispossess the Canaanites.”
text.DRB: but thou shalt pass to the mountain, and shalt cut and make glades for thee to inhabit: and mayest proceed farther, when thou hast subverted the Chananite, whom thou sayest to have iron chariots, and to be very strong.

text.NRSV: Then the whole congregation of the Israelites assembled at Shiloh, and set up the tent of meeting there. The land lay subdued before them.
text.Rotherham: Then were gathered together all the assembly of the sons of Israel at Shiloh, and they set up there, the tent of meeting,—the land, having been subdued before them.
text.KJV: And the whole congregation of the children of Israel assembled together at Shiloh, and set up the tabernacle of the congregation there. And the land was subdued before them.
text.NABRE: Chapter 18 The whole community of the Israelites assembled at Shiloh, where they set up the tent of meeting; and the land was subdued before them.
text.DRB: And all the children of Israel were assembled in Silo, and there they pitched the tabernacle of the testimony, & the Land was subdued to them.

text.NRSV: There remained among the Israelites seven tribes whose inheritance had not yet been apportioned.
text.Rotherham: But there remained among the sons of Israel, to whom had not been apportioned their inheritance,—seven tribes.
text.KJV: And there remained among the children of Israel seven tribes, which had not yet received their inheritance.
text.NABRE: The Seven Remaining Portions. There remained seven tribes among the Israelites that had not yet received their heritage.
text.DRB: But there remained seven tribes of the children of Israel, which as yet had not received their possessions.

text.NRSV: So Joshua said to the Israelites, “How long will you be slack about going in and taking possession of the land that the Lord, the God of your ancestors, has given you?
text.Rotherham: So Joshua said unto the sons of Israel,—How long, will ye be too slothful, to enter in and take possession of the land, which Yahweh God of your fathers, hath given unto you?
text.KJV: And Joshua said unto the children of Israel, How long are ye slack to go to possess the land, which the Lord God of your fathers hath given you?
text.NABRE: Joshua therefore said to the Israelites, “How much longer will you put off taking steps to possess the land which the Lord, the God of your ancestors, has given you?
text.DRB: To whom Josue said: How long are you slack with cowardness, and enter not to posses the Land, which our Lord the God of your fathers hath given you?

text.NRSV: Provide three men from each tribe, and I will send them out that they may begin to go throughout the land, writing a description of it with a view to their inheritances. Then come back to me.
text.Rotherham: Set forth for you three men for each tribe,—that I may send them, that they may arise—and go up and down in the land—and map it out as required for their inheritance, and come in unto me.
text.KJV: Give out from among you three men for each tribe: and I will send them, and they shall rise, and go through the land, and describe it according to the inheritance of them; and they shall come again to me.
text.NABRE: Choose three representatives from each of your tribes; I will send them to go throughout the land and describe it for purposes of acquiring their heritage. When they return to me
text.DRB: Choose of every tribe three men, that I may send them, and they may go and circuit the Land, and mark it out according to the number of every multitude: and report unto me that which they have marked out.

text.NRSV: They shall divide it into seven portions, Judah continuing in its territory on the south, and the house of Joseph in their territory on the north.
text.Rotherham: So shall they apportion it for themselves into seven parts,—Let, Judah, stay upon his boundary, on the south, and, the house of Joseph, stay upon their boundary, on the north,
text.KJV: And they shall divide it into seven parts: Judah shall abide in their coast on the south, and the house of Joseph shall abide in their coasts on the north.
text.NABRE: you shall divide it into seven parts. Judah is to retain its territory in the south, and the house of Joseph its territory in the north.
text.DRB: Divide unto you the Land into seven parts: let Judas be in the bounds on the south quarter, and the house of Joseph on the North.

text.NRSV: You shall describe the land in seven divisions and bring the description here to me; and I will cast lots for you here before the Lord our God.
text.Rotherham: but, ye, shall map out the [rest of the] land, into seven parts,—and bring in [your descriptions] unto me, here,—then will I cast lots for you here, before Yahweh our God.
text.KJV: Ye shall therefore describe the land into seven parts, and bring the description hither to me, that I may cast lots for you here before the Lord our God.
text.NABRE: You shall bring to me here the description of the land in seven sections. I will then cast lots for you here before the Lord, our God.
text.DRB: The Land in the midst between these mark out into seven parts: and you shall come hither to me, that before our Lord your God I may cast the lot for you:

text.NRSV: The Levites have no portion among you, for the priesthood of the Lord is their heritage; and Gad and Reuben and the half-tribe of Manasseh have received their inheritance beyond the Jordan eastward, which Moses the servant of the Lord gave them.”
text.Rotherham: For Levi hath no portion in your midst, for, the priesthood of Yahweh, is his inheritance,—and, Gad, and Reuben, and the half tribe of Manasseh, have received their inheritance, beyond the Jordan on the east, which Moses the servant of Yahweh gave unto them.
text.KJV: But the Levites have no part among you; for the priesthood of the Lord is their inheritance: and Gad, and Reuben, and half the tribe of Manasseh, have received their inheritance beyond Jordan on the east, which Moses the servant of the Lord gave them.
text.NABRE: For the Levites have no share among you, because the priesthood of the Lord is their heritage; while Gad, Reuben, and the half-tribe of Manasseh have already received the heritage east of the Jordan which Moses, the servant of the Lord, gave them.”
text.DRB: for the Levites' part is not among you, but the priesthood of our Lord is their inheritance. And Gad and Ruben, and the half tribe of Manasses had now received their possessions beyond Jordan at the East side: which Moyses the servant of our Lord gave them.

text.NRSV: So the men started on their way; and Joshua charged those who went to write the description of the land, saying, “Go throughout the land and write a description of it, and come back to me; and I will cast lots for you here before the Lord in Shiloh.”
text.Rotherham: So the men arose, and went,—and Joshua commanded them who were going, to map out the land, saying—Take your journey and go up and down in the land, and map it out, and return unto me, and, here, will I cast lots for you before Yahweh, in Shiloh.
text.KJV: And the men arose, and went away: and Joshua charged them that went to describe the land, saying, Go and walk through the land, and describe it, and come again to me, that I may here cast lots for you before the Lord in Shiloh.
text.NABRE: When those who were to describe the land were ready for their journey, Joshua commanded them, “Go throughout the land and describe it; return to me and I will cast lots for you here before the Lord in Shiloh.”
text.DRB: And when the men were risen up, that they might go to mark out the land, Josue commanded them, saying: Circuit the Land and mark it out, and return to me: that here before our Lord, in Silo I may cast the lot for you.

text.NRSV: So the men went and traversed the land and set down in a book a description of it by towns in seven divisions; then they came back to Joshua in the camp at Shiloh,
text.Rotherham: So the men went, and passed through the land, and mapped it out by cities, into seven parts, upon a scroll,—and came in unto Joshua, unto the camp, in Shiloh.
text.KJV: And the men went and passed through the land, and described it by cities into seven parts in a book, and came again to Joshua to the host at Shiloh.
text.NABRE: So they went through the land, described its cities in writing in seven sections, and returned to Joshua in the camp at Shiloh.
text.DRB: They therefore went on: and going over it, divided it into seven parts, writing it in a volume. And they returned to Josue into the camp in Silo.

text.NRSV: and Joshua cast lots for them in Shiloh before the Lord; and there Joshua apportioned the land to the Israelites, to each a portion.
text.Rotherham: And Joshua cast for them lots, in Shiloh, before Yahweh,—and Joshua there apportioned the land unto the sons of Israel, according to their portions.
text.KJV: And Joshua cast lots for them in Shiloh before the Lord: and there Joshua divided the land unto the children of Israel according to their divisions.
text.NABRE: Joshua then cast lots for them before the Lord in Shiloh, and divided up the land for the Israelites into their separate shares.
text.DRB: Who did cast lots before our Lord in Silo, and divided the Land to the children of Israel into seven parts.

text.NRSV: The lot of the tribe of Benjamin according to its families came up, and the territory allotted to it fell between the tribe of Judah and the tribe of Joseph.
text.Rotherham: Then came up the lot of the tribe of the sons of Benjamin, by their families,—and the boundary of their lot came out, between the sons of Judah and the sons of Joseph.
text.KJV: And the lot of the tribe of the children of Benjamin came up according to their families: and the coast of their lot came forth between the children of Judah and the children of Joseph.
text.NABRE: Benjamin. One lot fell to the tribe of Benjaminites by their clans. The territory allotted them lay between the descendants of Judah and those of Joseph.
text.DRB: And first came up the lot of the children of Benjamin by their families, to possess the Land between the children of Judas and the children of Joseph.

text.NRSV: On the north side their boundary began at the Jordan; then the boundary goes up to the slope of Jericho on the north, then up through the hill country westward; and it ends at the wilderness of Beth-aven.
text.Rotherham: And so their boundary, on the north border, was from the Jordan,—and the boundary goeth up unto the side of Jericho on the north, and goeth up in the hill country, westward, and the extensions thereof are towards the wilderness of Beth-aven;
text.KJV: And their border on the north side was from Jordan; and the border went up to the side of Jericho on the north side, and went up through the mountains westward; and the goings out thereof were at the wilderness of Beth–aven.
text.NABRE: Their northern boundary began at the Jordan and went over the northern flank of Jericho, up westward into the mountains, until it reached the wilderness of Beth-aven.
text.DRB: And their border was against the North from Jordan: going forward by the side of Jericho on the North quarter, and thence Westward rising up unto the mountains, and reaching to the wilderness of Bethaven,

text.NRSV: From there the boundary passes along southward in the direction of Luz, to the slope of Luz (that is, Bethel), then the boundary goes down to Ataroth-addar, on the mountain that lies south of Lower Beth-horon.
text.Rotherham: and the boundary passeth over from thence towards Luz, to the side of Luz, southwards, the same, is Bethel,—and the boundary goeth down to Ataroth-addah, by the mountain that is on the south of Beth-horon the nether;
text.KJV: And the border went over from thence toward Luz, to the side of Luz, which is Beth–el, southward; and the border descended to Ataroth–adar, near the hill that lieth on the south side of the nether Beth–horon.
text.NABRE: From there it crossed over to the southern flank of Luz (that is, Bethel). Then it ran down to Ataroth-addar, on the mountaintop south of Lower Beth-horon.
text.DRB: and passing through by Luza to the South, the same is Bethel: and goeth down into Ataroth addar unto the mountain, that is on the South of Beth-horon the lower.

text.NRSV: Then the boundary goes in another direction, turning on the western side southward from the mountain that lies to the south, opposite Beth-horon, and it ends at Kiriath-baal (that is, Kiriath-jearim), a town belonging to the tribe of Judah. This forms the western side.
text.Rotherham: then turneth the boundary and goeth round the west border, southward, from the mountain which faceth Beth-horon, southward, and so the extensions thereof are unto Kiriath-baal—the same, is Kiriath-jearim, a city of the sons of Judah,—this, is the west border.
text.KJV: And the border was drawn thence, and compassed the corner of the sea southward, from the hill that lieth before Beth–horon southward; and the goings out thereof were at Kirjath–baal, which is Kirjath–jearim, a city of the children of Judah: this was the west quarter.
text.NABRE: For the western border, the boundary line swung south from the mountaintop opposite Beth-horon until it reached Kiriath-baal (that is, Kiriath-jearim; this city belonged to the Judahites). This was the western boundary.
text.DRB: And it bendeth compassing against the sea, Southward of the mountain that looketh to Beth-horon against the South: and the issues thereof are into Cariath baal, which is called also Cariathiarim, a city of the children of Judas. This is their coast against the sea, toward the West.

text.NRSV: The southern side begins at the outskirts of Kiriath-jearim; and the boundary goes from there to Ephron, to the spring of the Waters of Nephtoah;
text.Rotherham: And, the border southward, is from the uttermost part of Kiriath-jearim,—and the boundary goeth out westward, yea it goeth out unto the fountain of the waters of Nephtoah;
text.KJV: And the south quarter was from the end of Kirjath–jearim, and the border went out on the west, and went out to the well of waters of Nephtoah:
text.NABRE: The southern boundary began at the limits of Kiriath-jearim and projected to the spring at Nephtoah.
text.DRB: But on the South from part of Cariathiarim the border issueth forth against the sea, and cometh to the fountain of the waters of Nephtoa.

text.NRSV: then the boundary goes down to the border of the mountain that overlooks the valley of the son of Hinnom, which is at the north end of the valley of Rephaim; and it then goes down the valley of Hinnom, south of the slope of the Jebusites, and downward to En-rogel;
text.Rotherham: and the boundary goeth down to the uttermost part of the mountain which faceth the valley of the son of Hinnom, which is in the Vale of Giants, northward,—and descendeth the valley of Hinnom, unto the side of the Jebusite, southward, and then descendeth to En-rogel;
text.KJV: And the border came down to the end of the mountain that lieth before the valley of the son of Hinnom, and which is in the valley of the giants on the north, and descended to the valley of Hinnom, to the side of Jebusi on the south, and descended to En–rogel,
text.NABRE: It went down to the edge of the mountain on the north of the Valley of Rephaim, where it faces the Valley of Ben-hinnom; and continuing down the Valley of Hinnom along the southern flank of the Jebusites, reached En-rogel.
text.DRB: And it goeth down into part of the mountain that looketh toward the Valley of the children of Ennom: and is against the North quarter in the uttermost part of the Valley Raphaim. And it goeth down into Gehennom (that is, the valley of Ennom) by the side of the Jebusite to the South: and cometh to the Fountain of Rogel,

text.NRSV: then it bends in a northerly direction going on to En-shemesh, and from there goes to Geliloth, which is opposite the ascent of Adummim; then it goes down to the Stone of Bohan, Reuben’s son;
text.Rotherham: and it turneth on the north, and goeth out at En-shemesh, and goeth out unto Geliloth, which is over against the ascent of Adummim,—and goeth down by the Stone of Bohan, son of Reuben;
text.KJV: And was drawn from the north, and went forth to En–shemesh, and went forth toward Geliloth, which is over against the going up of Adummim, and descended to the stone of Bohan the son of Reuben,
text.NABRE: Inclining to the north, it extended to En-shemesh, and thence to Geliloth, opposite the pass of Adummim. Then it dropped to Eben-Bohan-ben-Reuben,
text.DRB: passing to the North, and going forth to Ensemes, that is to say, the fountain of the sun:

text.NRSV: and passing on to the north of the slope of Beth-arabah it goes down to the Arabah;
text.Rotherham: and passeth along unto the side over against the Arabah, northward,—and goeth down toward the Arabah;
text.KJV: And passed along toward the side over against Arabah northward, and went down unto Arabah:
text.NABRE: across the northern flank of the Arabah overlook, down into the Arabah.
text.DRB: and it passeth unto the little hills, that are against the ascent of Adommim: and goeth down to Abenboen, that is, the stone of Boen the son of Ruben: and it passeth on the North side to the champion countries: and goeth down into the plain,

text.NRSV: then the boundary passes on to the north of the slope of Beth-hoglah; and the boundary ends at the northern bay of the Dead Sea, at the south end of the Jordan: this is the southern border.
text.Rotherham: and the boundary passeth along unto the side of Beth-hoglah, northward, and so the extensions of the boundary are unto the bay of the Salt Sea, northward, unto the end of the Jordan, southward,—This, is the south boundary.
text.KJV: And the border passed along to the side of Beth–hoglah northward: and the outgoings of the border were at the north bay of the salt sea at the south end of Jordan: this was the south coast.
text.NABRE: From there the boundary continued across the northern flank of Beth-hoglah and extended northward to the tongue of the Salt Sea, toward the southern end of the Jordan. This was the southern boundary.
text.DRB: and passeth by against the North of Bethagla: and the issues thereof are against the brink of the most salt sea on the North in the end of Jordan to the south quarter:

text.NRSV: The Jordan forms its boundary on the eastern side. This is the inheritance of the tribe of Benjamin, according to its families, boundary by boundary all around.
text.Rotherham: And, the Jordan itself, boundeth it as an eastward border. This, is the inheritance of the sons of Benjamin, by the boundaries thereof round about, by their families.
text.KJV: And Jordan was the border of it on the east side. This was the inheritance of the children of Benjamin, by the coasts thereof round about, according to their families.
text.NABRE: The Jordan bounded it on the east. This was how the heritage of the Benjaminites by their clans was bounded on all sides.
text.DRB: which is the border thereof on the East. This is the possession of the children of Benjamin by their borders round about, and their families.

text.NRSV: Now the towns of the tribe of Benjamin according to their families were Jericho, Beth-hoglah, Emek-keziz,
text.Rotherham: So then the cities of the tribe of the sons of Benjamin, by their families, are,—Jericho and Beth-hoglah, and Emekkeziz,
text.KJV: Now the cities of the tribe of the children of Benjamin according to their families were Jericho, and Beth–hoglah, and the valley of Keziz,
text.NABRE: Now the cities belonging to the tribe of the Benjaminites by their clans were: Jericho, Beth-hoglah, Emek-keziz,
text.DRB: And their cities were, Jericho and Beth hagla and Vallis Casis,

text.NRSV: Beth-arabah, Zemaraim, Bethel,
text.Rotherham: and Beth-arabah and Zemaraim, and Bethel,
text.KJV: And Beth–arabah, and Zemaraim, and Beth–el,
text.NABRE: Beth-arabah, Zemaraim, Bethel,
text.DRB: Beth Araba and Samaraim and Bethel,

text.NRSV: Avvim, Parah, Ophrah,
text.Rotherham: and Avvim and Parah and Ophrah,
text.KJV: And Avim, and Parah, and Ophrah,
text.NABRE: Avvim, Parah, Ophra,
text.DRB: and Avim and Aphara and Ophera,

text.NRSV: Chephar-ammoni, Ophni, and Geba—twelve towns with their villages:
text.Rotherham: and Chephar-ammoni and Ophni, and Geba,—twelve cities, with their villages:
text.KJV: And Chephar–haammonai, and Ophni, and Gaba; twelve cities with their villages:
text.NABRE: Chephar-ammoni, Ophni, and Geba; twelve cities and their villages.
text.DRB: Town Emona and Ophni and Gabee: twelve cities, & their towns.

text.NRSV: Gibeon, Ramah, Beeroth,
text.Rotherham: Gibeon and Ramah, and Beeroth,
text.KJV: Gibeon, and Ramah, and Beeroth,
text.NABRE: Also Gibeon, Ramah, Beeroth,
text.DRB: Gabaon and Rama and Beroth,

text.NRSV: Mizpeh, Chephirah, Mozah,
text.Rotherham: and Mizpeh and Chephirah, and Mozah,
text.KJV: And Mizpeh, and Chephirah, and Mozah,
text.NABRE: Mizpeh, Chephirah, Mozah,
text.DRB: and Mesphe, and Caphara, and Amosa,

text.NRSV: Rekem, Irpeel, Taralah,
text.Rotherham: and Rekem and Irpeel, and Taralah,
text.KJV: And Rekem, and Irpeel, and Taralah,
text.NABRE: Rekem, Irpeel, Taralah,
text.DRB: and Recem, Iarephel and Tharela,

text.NRSV: Zela, Haeleph, Jebus (that is, Jerusalem), Gibeah and Kiriath-jearim—fourteen towns with their villages. This is the inheritance of the tribe of Benjamin according to its families.
text.Rotherham: and Zelah, Eleph, and the Jebusite, the same, is Jerusalem, Gibeath [and] Kiriath, fourteen cities, with their villages. This, is the inheritance of the song of Benjamin, by their families.
text.KJV: And Zelah, Eleph, and Jebusi, which is Jerusalem, Gibeath, and Kirjath; fourteen cities with their villages. This is the inheritance of the children of Benjamin according to their families.
text.NABRE: Zela, Haeleph, the Jebusite city (that is, Jerusalem), Gibeah, and Kiriath; fourteen cities and their villages. This was the heritage of the clans of Benjaminites.
text.DRB: and Sela, Eleph, and Jebus, which is Jerusalem, Gabaath and Cariath: fourteen cities, and their towns. This is the possession of the children of Benjamin by their families.

text.NRSV: The second lot came out for Simeon, for the tribe of Simeon, according to its families; its inheritance lay within the inheritance of the tribe of Judah.
text.Rotherham: And the second lot came out, for Simeon, for the tribe of the sons of Simeon, by their families,—and their inheritance was in the midst of the inheritance of the sons of Judah.
text.KJV: And the second lot came forth to Simeon, even for the tribe of the children of Simeon according to their families: and their inheritance was within the inheritance of the children of Judah.
text.NABRE: Chapter 19 Simeon. The second lot fell to Simeon. The heritage of the tribe of Simeonites by their clans lay within that of the Judahites.
text.DRB: And the second lot came forth of the children of Simeon by their kindreds: and their inheritance was

text.NRSV: It had for its inheritance Beer-sheba, Sheba, Moladah,
text.Rotherham: And they had for their inheritance,—Beer-sheba or Sheba, and Moladah,
text.KJV: And they had in their inheritance Beer–sheba, Sheba, and Moladah,
text.NABRE: For their heritage they received Beer-sheba, Shema, Moladah,
text.DRB: in the midst of the possession of the children of Judas: Bersabee and Sabee and Molada,

text.NRSV: Hazar-shual, Balah, Ezem,
text.Rotherham: and Hazar-shual, and Balah, and Ezem,
text.KJV: And Hazar–shual, and Balah, and Azem,
text.NABRE: Hazar-shual, Balah, Ezem,
text.DRB: and Hasersual, Bala and Asem,

text.NRSV: Eltolad, Bethul, Hormah,
text.Rotherham: and Eltolad and Bethul, and Hormah,
text.KJV: And Eltolad, and Bethul, and Hormah,
text.NABRE: Eltolad, Bethul, Hormah,
text.DRB: and Eltholad, Bethul and Harma,

text.NRSV: Ziklag, Beth-marcaboth, Hazar-susah,
text.Rotherham: and Ziklag and Beth-marcaboth, and Hazar-susah,
text.KJV: And Ziklag, and Beth–marcaboth, and Hazar–susah,
text.NABRE: Ziklag, Bethmar-caboth, Hazar-susah,
text.DRB: and Siceleg and Betmarchaboth and Hasersusa,

text.NRSV: Beth-lebaoth, and Sharuhen—thirteen towns with their villages;
text.Rotherham: and Beth-lebaoth, and Sharuhen,—thirteen cities, with their villages:
text.KJV: And Beth–lebaoth, and Sharuhen; thirteen cities and their villages:
text.NABRE: Beth-lebaoth, and Sharuhen; thirteen cities and their villages.
text.DRB: and Bethlebaoth & Sarohen: thirteen cities, and their towns:

text.NRSV: Ain, Rimmon, Ether, and Ashan—four towns with their villages;
text.Rotherham: Ain, Rimmon, and Ether and Ashan,—four cities, with their villages,
text.KJV: Ain, Remmon, and Ether, and Ashan; four cities and their villages:
text.NABRE: Also Ain, Rimmon, Ether, and Ashan; four cities and their villages,
text.DRB: Ain and Remmon and Athor and Asan: four cities, and their towns:

text.NRSV: together with all the villages all around these towns as far as Baalath-beer, Ramah of the Negeb. This was the inheritance of the tribe of Simeon according to its families.
text.Rotherham: and all the villages that were round about these cities, as far as Baalath-beer, Ramah of the South. This, is the inheritance of the tribe of the sons of Simeon, by their families:
text.KJV: And all the villages that were round about these cities to Baalath–beer, Ramath of the south. This is the inheritance of the tribe of the children of Simeon according to their families.
text.NABRE: besides all the villages around these cities as far as Baalath-beer (that is, Ramoth-negeb). This was the heritage of the tribe of the Simeonites by their clans.
text.DRB: all the little towns round about these cities unto Baalath Beer Ramath against the south quarter. This is the inheritance of the children of Simeon according to their kindreds,

text.NRSV: The inheritance of the tribe of Simeon formed part of the territory of Judah; because the portion of the tribe of Judah was too large for them, the tribe of Simeon obtained an inheritance within their inheritance.
text.Rotherham: Out of the portion of the sons of Judah, is the inheritance of the sons of Simeon,—for it came to pass that what was allotted to the sons of Judah, was too much for them, therefore did the sons of Simeon receive an inheritance in the midst of their inheritance.
text.KJV: Out of the portion of the children of Judah was the inheritance of the children of Simeon: for the part of the children of Judah was too much for them: therefore the children of Simeon had their inheritance within the inheritance of them.
text.NABRE: This heritage of the Simeonites was within the confines of the Judahites; for since the portion of the latter was too large for them, the Simeonites obtained their heritage within it.
text.DRB: in the possession and cord of the children of Judas: because it was greater. And therefore the children of Simeon possessed in the midst of their inheritance.

text.NRSV: The third lot came up for the tribe of Zebulun, according to its families. The boundary of its inheritance reached as far as Sarid;
text.Rotherham: Then came up the third lot, for the sons of Zebulun, by their families,—and the boundary of their inheritance was as far as Sarid.
text.KJV: And the third lot came up for the children of Zebulun according to their families: and the border of their inheritance was unto Sarid:
text.NABRE: Zebulun. The third lot fell to the Zebulunites by their clans. The boundary of their heritage was at Sarid.
text.DRB: And the third lot fell of the children of Zabulon by their kindreds: and the border of their possession was made as far as Sarid.

text.NRSV: then its boundary goes up westward, and on to Maralah, and touches Dabbesheth, then the wadi that is east of Jokneam;
text.Rotherham: And their boundary goeth up westward, even towards Maralah, and toucheth Dabbesheth,—and reacheth unto the ravine that faceth Jokneam;
text.KJV: And their border went up toward the sea, and Maralah, and reached to Dabbasheth, and reached to the river that is before Jokneam;
text.NABRE: Their boundary went up west and through Mareal, reaching Dabbesheth and the wadi that is near Jokneam.
text.DRB: And it went up from the sea and Merala, and came into Debbaseth: as far as the torrent, which is against Ieconam.

text.NRSV: from Sarid it goes in the other direction eastward toward the sunrise to the boundary of Chisloth-tabor; from there it goes to Daberath, then up to Japhia;
text.Rotherham: and turneth back from Sarid, eastward, toward the rising of the sun, upon the boundary of Chisloth-tabor,—and goeth out unto Daberath, and ascendeth Japhia;
text.KJV: And turned from Sarid eastward toward the sunrising unto the border of Chisloth–tabor, and then goeth out to Daberath, and goeth up to Japhia,
text.NABRE: From Sarid eastward it ran to the district of Chisloth-tabor, on to Daberath, and up to Japhia.
text.DRB: And it returneth from Sared against the East into the ends of Ceseleth thabor: and it goeth out to Dabereth, and riseth up against Iaphie.

text.NRSV: from there it passes along on the east toward the sunrise to Gath-hepher, to Eth-kazin, and going on to Rimmon it bends toward Neah;
text.Rotherham: and, from thence, it passed along in front on the east, towards Gath-hepher, towards Eth-kazin,—and goeth out at Rimmon which turneth about towards Neah;
text.KJV: And from thence passeth on along on the east to Gittah–hepher, to Ittah–kazin, and goeth out to Remmon–methoar to Neah;
text.NABRE: From there it continued eastward to Gath-hepher and to Eth-kazin, extended to Rimmon, and turned to Neah.
text.DRB: And thence it passeth along to the east side of Geth hepher and Thacasim: and goeth out into Remmon, Amthar and Noa.

text.NRSV: then on the north the boundary makes a turn to Hannathon, and it ends at the valley of Iphtah-el;
text.Rotherham: and the boundary goeth round it, on the north to Hannathon,—and so the extensions thereof are the valley of Iphtah-el;
text.KJV: And the border compasseth it on the north side to Hannathon: and the outgoings thereof are in the valley of Jiphthah–el:
text.NABRE: Skirting north of Hannathon, the boundary ended at the valley of Iphtahel.
text.DRB: And it compasseth to the North of Hanathon: & the issues thereof are the valley Iephtahel,

text.NRSV: and Kattath, Nahalal, Shimron, Idalah, and Bethlehem—twelve towns with their villages.
text.Rotherham: and Kattath and Nahalal, and Shimron, and Idalah and Beth-lehem,—twelve cities, with their villages.
text.KJV: And Kattath, and Nahallal, and Shimron, and Idalah, and Beth–lehem: twelve cities with their villages.
text.NABRE: Thus, with Kattath, Nahalal, Shimron, Idalah, and Bethlehem, there were twelve cities and their villages.
text.DRB: & Cateth & Naalol & Semeron & Ierala & Bethlehem: twelve cities, & their towns.

text.NRSV: This is the inheritance of the tribe of Zebulun, according to its families—these towns with their villages.
text.Rotherham: This, is the inheritance of the sons of Zebulun, by their families,—these cities, with their villages.
text.KJV: This is the inheritance of the children of Zebulun according to their families, these cities with their villages.
text.NABRE: This was the heritage of the Zebulunites by their clans, these cities and their villages.
text.DRB: This is the inheritance of the tribe of the children of Zabulon by their kindreds, the cities and their little towns.

text.NRSV: The fourth lot came out for Issachar, for the tribe of Issachar, according to its families.
text.Rotherham: For Issachar, came out the fourth lot,—for the sons of Issachar, by their families.
text.KJV: And the fourth lot came out to Issachar, for the children of Issachar according to their families.
text.NABRE: Issachar. The fourth lot fell to Issachar. The territory of the Issacharites by their clans
text.DRB: The fourth lot came forth to Issachar by their kindreds.

text.NRSV: Its territory included Jezreel, Chesulloth, Shunem,
text.Rotherham: And their boundary was,—Jezreel and Chesulloth, and Shunem,
text.KJV: And their border was toward Jezreel, and Chesulloth, and Shunem,
text.NABRE: included Jezreel, Chesulloth, Shunem,
text.DRB: And his inheritance was Jezrael and Casaloth and Sunem,

text.NRSV: Hapharaim, Shion, Anaharath,
text.Rotherham: and Hapharaim and Shion, and Anaharath,
text.KJV: And Hapharaim, and Shion, and Anaharath,
text.NABRE: Hapharaim, Shion, Anaharath,
text.DRB: and Hapharaim and Sehon, and Anaharath

text.NRSV: Rabbith, Kishion, Ebez,
text.Rotherham: and Rabbith and Kishion, and Ebez,
text.KJV: And Rabbith, and Kishion, and Abez,
text.NABRE: Rabbith, Kishion, Ebez,
text.DRB: and Rabboth & Cesion, Abes,

text.NRSV: Remeth, En-gannim, En-haddah, Beth-pazzez;
text.Rotherham: and Remeth, and En-gannim, and Enhaddah, and Beth-pazzez;
text.KJV: And Remeth, and En–gannim, and En–haddah, and Beth–pazzez;
text.NABRE: Remeth, En-gannim, En-haddah, and Beth-pazzez.
text.DRB: & Rameth, and Engannim, and Enhadda and Bethpheses.

text.NRSV: the boundary also touches Tabor, Shahazumah, and Beth-shemesh, and its boundary ends at the Jordan—sixteen towns with their villages.
text.Rotherham: and the boundary toucheth Tabor and Shahazumah, and Beth-shemesh, and so the extensions of their boundary are to the Jordan,—sixteen cities, with their villages.
text.KJV: And the coast reacheth to Tabor, and Shahazimah, and Beth–shemesh; and the outgoings of their border were at Jordan: sixteen cities with their villages.
text.NABRE: The boundary reached Tabor, Shahazumah, and Beth-shemesh, ending at the Jordan: sixteen cities and their villages.
text.DRB: And the border thereof cometh to Thabor & Sehesema and Bethsames: and their issues were Jordan: sixteen cities, and their towns.

text.NRSV: This is the inheritance of the tribe of Issachar, according to its families—the towns with their villages.
text.Rotherham: This, is the inheritance of the tribe of the sons of Issachar, by their families,—the cities, with their villages.
text.KJV: This is the inheritance of the tribe of the children of Issachar according to their families, the cities and their villages.
text.NABRE: This was the heritage of the Issacharites by their clans, these cities and their villages.
text.DRB: This is the possession of Issachar by their kindreds, the cities, and their little towns.

text.NRSV: The fifth lot came out for the tribe of Asher according to its families.
text.Rotherham: Then came out the fifth lot, for the tribe of the sons of Asher, by their families.
text.KJV: And the fifth lot came out for the tribe of the children of Asher according to their families.
text.NABRE: Asher. The fifth lot fell to the Asherites by their clans.
text.DRB: And the fifth lot fell to the tribe of the children of Aser by their kindreds:

text.NRSV: Its boundary included Helkath, Hali, Beten, Achshaph,
text.Rotherham: And their boundary was,—Helkath and Hali, and Beten and Achshaph,
text.KJV: And their border was Helkath, and Hali, and Beten, and Achshaph,
text.NABRE: Their territory included Helkath, Hali, Beten, Achshaph,
text.DRB: and their border was Halcath and Chali and Beten and Axaph,

text.NRSV: Allammelech, Amad, and Mishal; on the west it touches Carmel and Shihor-libnath,
text.Rotherham: and Allam-melech and Amad, and Mishal,—and it toucheth Carmel to the west, and Shihor-libnath;
text.KJV: And Alammelech, and Amad, and Misheal; and reacheth to Carmel westward, and to Shihor–libnath;
text.NABRE: Allammelech, Amad, and Mishal, and reached Carmel on the west, and Shihor-libnath.
text.DRB: and Elmelec and Amaad and Messal: and it reacheth to Carmel of the sea and Sihor and Labanath.

text.NRSV: then it turns eastward, goes to Beth-dagon, and touches Zebulun and the valley of Iphtah-el northward to Beth-emek and Neiel; then it continues in the north to Cabul,
text.Rotherham: and it turneth toward sun-rise—to Beth-dagon, and toucheth Zebulun and the valley of Iphtah-el northward, and Beth-emek, and Neiel; and goeth out unto Cabul, on the left,
text.KJV: And turneth toward the sunrising to Beth–dagon, and reacheth to Zebulun, and to the valley of Jiphthah–el toward the north side of Beth–emek, and Neiel, and goeth out to Cabul on the left hand,
text.NABRE: In the other direction, it ran eastward of Beth-dagon, reached Zebulun and the valley of Iphtahel; then north of Beth-emek and Neiel, it extended northward to Cabul,
text.DRB: And it returneth against the east of Bethdagon: and passeth along to Zabulon and the Valley Iephthael against the North into Bethemec and Nehiel. And it goeth out to the left side of Cabul,

text.NRSV: Ebron, Rehob, Hammon, Kanah, as far as Sidon the Great;
text.Rotherham: and Ebron and Rehob, and Hammon and Kanah,—as far as Zidon the populous;
text.KJV: And Hebron, and Rehob, and Hammon, and Kanah, even unto great Zidon;
text.NABRE: Ebron, Rehob, Hammon, and Kanah, near Greater Sidon.
text.DRB: and Abran and Rohob and Hamon and Cana, as far as great Sidon.

text.NRSV: then the boundary turns to Ramah, reaching to the fortified city of Tyre; then the boundary turns to Hosah, and it ends at the sea; Mahalab, Achzib,
text.Rotherham: and the boundary turneth to Ramah, and as far as the city of the fortress of Tyre,—then the boundary turneth to Hosah, and so the extensions thereof are, on the west, from Hebel to Achzib;
text.KJV: And then the coast turneth to Ramah, and to the strong city Tyre; and the coast turneth to Hosah; and the outgoings thereof are at the sea from the coast to Achzib:
text.NABRE: Then the boundary turned back to Ramah and to the fortress city of Tyre; thence it cut back to Hosah and ended at the sea. Thus, with Mahalab, Achzib,
text.DRB: And it returneth into Horma unto the very well fenced city Tyre, & unto Hosa: and the issues thereof shall be into the sea from the cord of Achziba:

text.NRSV: Ummah, Aphek, and Rehob—twenty-two towns with their villages.
text.Rotherham: Ummah also and Aphek, and Rehob,—twenty-two cities, with their villages,
text.KJV: Ummah also, and Aphek, and Rehob: twenty and two cities with their villages.
text.NABRE: Ummah, Acco, Aphek, and Rehob, there were twenty-two cities and their villages.
text.DRB: and Amma and Aphec & Rohob, cities twenty two, and their towns.

text.NRSV: This is the inheritance of the tribe of Asher according to its families—these towns with their villages.
text.Rotherham: This, is the inheritance of the tribe of the sons of Asher, by their families,—these cities, with their villages.
text.KJV: This is the inheritance of the tribe of the children of Asher according to their families, these cities with their villages.
text.NABRE: This was the heritage of the tribe of the Asherites by their clans, these cities and their villages.
text.DRB: This is the possession of the children of Aser by their kindreds, and the cities and their towns.

text.NRSV: The sixth lot came out for the tribe of Naphtali, for the tribe of Naphtali, according to its families.
text.Rotherham: For the sons of Naphtali, came out the sixth lot,—for the sons of Naphtali, by their families.
text.KJV: The sixth lot came out to the children of Naphtali, even for the children of Naphtali according to their families.
text.NABRE: Naphtali. The sixth lot fell to the Naphtalites.
text.DRB: Of the sons of Nephthali fell the sixth lot by their families:

text.NRSV: And its boundary ran from Heleph, from the oak in Zaanannim, and Adami-nekeb, and Jabneel, as far as Lakkum; and it ended at the Jordan;
text.Rotherham: And their boundary was from Heleph, from the terebinth of Bezaannim, and Adami-nekeb, and Jabneel, as far as Lakkum; and so the extensions thereof were to the Jordan;
text.KJV: And their coast was from Heleph, from Allon to Zaanannim, and Adami, Nekeb, and Jabneel, unto Lakum; and the outgoings thereof were at Jordan:
text.NABRE: Their boundary extended from Heleph, from the oak at Zaanannim, including Adami-nekeb and Jabneel, to Lakkum, and ended at the Jordan.
text.DRB: and the border began from Heleph and Elon into Saanaim, and Adami, which is Neceb, and Iebnael unto Lecum: and their issues unto Jordan:

text.NRSV: then the boundary turns westward to Aznoth-tabor, and goes from there to Hukkok, touching Zebulun at the south, and Asher on the west, and Judah on the east at the Jordan.
text.Rotherham: and the boundary turneth westward, to Aznoth-tabor, and goeth out from thence, toward Hukkok,—and toucheth Zebulun on the south, and, Asher, it toucheth on the west, and Judah, at the Jordan towards sunrise.
text.KJV: And then the coast turneth westward to Aznoth–tabor, and goeth out from thence to Hukkok, and reacheth to Zebulun on the south side, and reacheth to Asher on the west side, and to Judah upon Jordan toward the sunrising.
text.NABRE: In the opposite direction, westerly, it ran through Aznoth-tabor and from there extended to Hukkok; it reached Zebulun on the south, Asher on the west, and the Jordan on the east.
text.DRB: and the border returneth against the West into Azanotthabor, and thence goeth out into Hucuca, and passeth along into Zabulon against the South, and into Azer against the West, and into Juda unto Jordan against the rising of the sun.

text.NRSV: The fortified towns are Ziddim, Zer, Hammath, Rakkath, Chinnereth,
text.Rotherham: And, the fortified cities, are,—Ziddim, Zer, and Hammath, Rakkath and Chinnereth,
text.KJV: And the fenced cities are Ziddim, Zer, and Hammath, Rakkath, and Chinnereth,
text.NABRE: The fortified cities were Ziddim, Zer, Hammath, Rakkath, Chinnereth,
text.DRB: cities very well fenced, Assedim, Ser, and Emath, and Reccath and Cenereth,

text.NRSV: Adamah, Ramah, Hazor,
text.Rotherham: and Adamah and Ramah, and Hazor,
text.KJV: And Adamah, and Ramah, and Hazor,
text.NABRE: Adamah, Ramah, Hazor,
text.DRB: and Edema and Arama, Asor,

text.NRSV: Kedesh, Edrei, En-hazor,
text.Rotherham: and Kedesh and Edrei, and En-hazor,
text.KJV: And Kedesh, and Edrei, and En–hazor,
text.NABRE: Kedesh, Edrei, En-hazor,
text.DRB: and Cedes and Edrai, Enhasor

text.NRSV: Iron, Migdal-el, Horem, Beth-anath, and Beth-shemesh—nineteen towns with their villages.
text.Rotherham: and Iron and Migdal-el, Horem and Beth-anath, and Beth-shemesh,—nineteen cities, with their villages.
text.KJV: And Iron, and Migdal–el, Horem, and Beth–anath, and Beth–shemesh; nineteen cities with their villages.
text.NABRE: Yiron, Migdal-el, Horem, Beth-anath, and Beth-shemesh; nineteen cities and their villages.
text.DRB: and Ieron and Magdalel, Horem and Bethanath and Bethsames: nineteen cities and their towns.

text.NRSV: This is the inheritance of the tribe of Naphtali according to its families—the towns with their villages.
text.Rotherham: This, is the inheritance of the tribe of the sons of Naphtali, by their families,—the cities, with their villages.
text.KJV: This is the inheritance of the tribe of the children of Naphtali according to their families, the cities and their villages.
text.NABRE: This was the heritage of the tribe of the Naphtalites by their clans, these cities and their villages.
text.DRB: This is the possession of the tribe of the children of Nephthali by their kindreds, the cities and their towns.

text.NRSV: The seventh lot came out for the tribe of Dan, according to its families.
text.Rotherham: And, for the tribe of the sons of Dan, by their families, came out the seventh lot.
text.KJV: And the seventh lot came out for the tribe of the children of Dan according to their families.
text.NABRE: Dan. The seventh lot fell to the tribe of Danites by their clans.
text.DRB: To the tribe of the children of Dan by their families came forth the seventh lot:

text.NRSV: The territory of its inheritance included Zorah, Eshtaol, Ir-shemesh,
text.Rotherham: And the boundary of their inheritance was,—Zorah and Eshtaol, and Ir-shemesh,
text.KJV: And the coast of their inheritance was Zorah, and Eshtaol, and Ir–shemesh,
text.NABRE: Their heritage was the territory of Zorah, Eshtaol, Ir-shemesh,
text.DRB: and the border of their possession was Sara and Esthaol, & Hirsemes, that is the city of the sun.

text.NRSV: Shaalabbin, Aijalon, Ithlah,
text.Rotherham: and Shaalabbin and Aijalon, and Ithlah,
text.KJV: And Shaalabbin, and Ajalon, and Jethlah,
text.NABRE: Shaalabbin, Aijalon, Ithlah,
text.DRB: Selebin & Aialon and Iethela,

text.NRSV: Elon, Timnah, Ekron,
text.Rotherham: and Elon and Timnah, and Ekron,
text.KJV: And Elon, and Thimnathah, and Ekron,
text.NABRE: Elon, Timnah, Ekron,
text.DRB: Elon and Themna and Acron,

text.NRSV: Eltekeh, Gibbethon, Baalath,
text.Rotherham: and Eltekeh and Gibbethon, and Baalath,
text.KJV: And Eltekeh, and Gibbethon, and Baalath,
text.NABRE: Eltekoh, Gibbethon, Baalath,
text.DRB: Elthece, Gebbethon and Balaath,

text.NRSV: Jehud, Bene-berak, Gath-rimmon,
text.Rotherham: and Jehud and Beneberak, and Gath-rimmon,
text.KJV: And Jehud, and Bene–berak, and Gath–rimmon,
text.NABRE: Jehud, Bene-berak, Gath-rimmon,
text.DRB: and Jud and Bane and Barac and Gethremmon:

text.NRSV: Me-jarkon, and Rakkon at the border opposite Joppa.
text.Rotherham: and Me-jarkon, and Rakkon,—with the boundary over against Joppa.
text.KJV: And Me–jarkon, and Rakkon, with the border before Japho.
text.NABRE: Me-jarkon, and Rakkon, with the coast at Joppa.
text.DRB: and Meiarcon & Arecon, with the border that looketh toward Joppe,

text.NRSV: When the territory of the Danites was lost to them, the Danites went up and fought against Leshem, and after capturing it and putting it to the sword, they took possession of it and settled in it, calling Leshem, Dan, after their ancestor Dan.
text.Rotherham: And, when the boundary of the sons of Dan went out beyond these, then went up the sons of Dan and fought against Leshem, and captured it, and smote it with the edge of the sword, and took possession thereof, and dwelt therein, and they called Leshem—Dan, after the name of Dan their father,
text.KJV: And the coast of the children of Dan went out too little for them: therefore the children of Dan went up to fight against Leshem, and took it, and smote it with the edge of the sword, and possessed it, and dwelt therein, and called Leshem, Dan, after the name of Dan their father.
text.NABRE: But the territory of the Danites was too small for them; so the Danites marched up and attacked Leshem, which they captured and put to the sword. Once they had taken possession of Leshem, they dwelt there and named it after their ancestor Dan.
text.DRB: and is shut up with the same end. And the children of Dan went up, & fought against Lesem, and they took it: and they struck it in the edge of the sword, and possessed, and dwelt in it, calling the name of it Lesem Dan, by the name of Dan the father thereof.

text.NRSV: This is the inheritance of the tribe of Dan, according to their families—these towns with their villages.
text.Rotherham: This, is the inheritance of the tribe of the sons of Dan, by their families,—these cities, with their villages.
text.KJV: This is the inheritance of the tribe of the children of Dan according to their families, these cities with their villages.
text.NABRE: This was the heritage of the tribe of the Danites by their clans, these cities and their villages.
text.DRB: This is the possession of the tribe of the sons of Dan, by their kindreds, the cities and their towns:

text.NRSV: When they had finished distributing the several territories of the land as inheritances, the Israelites gave an inheritance among them to Joshua son of Nun.
text.Rotherham: When they had made an end of distributing the land by the boundaries thereof, then gave the sons of Israel an inheritance unto Joshua son of Nun, in their midst:
text.KJV: When they had made an end of dividing the land for inheritance by their coasts, the children of Israel gave an inheritance to Joshua the son of Nun among them:
text.NABRE: Joshua’s City. When the last of them had received the portions of the land they were to inherit, the Israelites assigned a heritage in their midst to Joshua, son of Nun.
text.DRB: And when he had made an end of dividing the Land by lot to every one by their tribes, the children of Israel gave possession to Josue the son of Nun in the midst of them,

text.NRSV: By command of the Lord they gave him the town that he asked for, Timnath-serah in the hill country of Ephraim; he rebuilt the town, and settled in it.
text.Rotherham: at the bidding of Yahweh, gave they unto him the city which he asked, even Timnath-serah, in the hill country of Ephraim,—and he built the city and dwelt therein.
text.KJV: According to the word of the Lord they gave him the city which he asked, even Timnath–serah in mount Ephraim: and he built the city, and dwelt therein.
text.NABRE: According to the command of the Lord, they gave him the city he requested, Timnah-serah in the mountain region of Ephraim. He rebuilt the city and made it his home.
text.DRB: according to the commandment of our Lord, the city which he requested, Thamnath Saraa in mount Ephraim: and he built the city, and dwelt in it.

text.NRSV: These are the inheritances that the priest Eleazar and Joshua son of Nun and the heads of the families of the tribes of the Israelites distributed by lot at Shiloh before the Lord, at the entrance of the tent of meeting. So they finished dividing the land.
text.Rotherham: These, are the inheritances which Eleazar the priest and Joshua son of Nun and the ancestral heads distributed for inheritance to the tribes of the sons of Israel, by lot, in Shiloh, before Yahweh, at the entrance of the tent of meeting,—so they made an end of apportioning the land.
text.KJV: These are the inheritances, which Eleazar the priest, and Joshua the son of Nun, and the heads of the fathers of the tribes of the children of Israel, divided for an inheritance by lot in Shiloh before the Lord, at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation. So they made an end of dividing the country.
text.NABRE: These are the heritages which Eleazar the priest, Joshua, son of Nun, and the heads of families in the tribes of the Israelites apportioned by lot in the presence of the Lord, at the door of the tent of meeting in Shiloh. Thus they finished dividing the land.
